races.race_name: Charlie Wilson Memorial Maiden Hurdle (GBB Race)

runners.name: Testing Patience
runners.spotlight: Going the right way judged on his second to an odds-on favourite at Fontwell (2m3f, good to soft) and has very solid claims.
runners.comment: Going the right way judged on his second to an odds-on favourite at Fontwell

runners.name: Mr Finch
runners.spotlight: Bought for £70,000 after his second in an Irish point; went from the front at Newbury last time (2m4f, good to soft) and ran fairly well behind two useful novices; this looks easier.
runners.comment: Ran fairly well last time behind two useful novices; this looks easier

runners.name: Lightfingered Jack
runners.spotlight: Hasn't kicked on from his encouraging debut (2m4f, good to soft) and probably needs to better that form to win this; however, it's still days and this gelding has a very appealing jumps pedigree.
runners.comment: Hasn't kicked on from his encouraging debut and probably needs to better that form

runners.name: Vanderflier
runners.spotlight: Bought for £155,000 after winning an Irish point; has finished weakly in both runs over hurdles (2m3f, heavy; 2m7f soft) but interesting that he's since had a wind operation.
runners.comment: Irish point winner; had a wind operation since finishing weakly in both hurdle runs

runners.name: Hay Max
runners.spotlight: Best run was the bumper debut at Bangor; beaten 26l+ in his subsequent races, the latest his hurdle debut at Sedgefield (2m4f, good).
runners.comment: Third on debut; beaten 26l+ in subsequent races, the latest his hurdle debut at Sedgefield

runners.name: Jorah d'Alma
runners.spotlight: Won a Ludlow bumper; 0-3 over hurdles but ran better with a tongue-tie added when a 15l third at Bangor (2m3f, good); probably needs to step up again and by some way to beat the best of these.
runners.comment: Improved third at Bangor but probably needs to step up again to beat the best of these

runners.name: Fullythermal
runners.spotlight: Well held in both his bumpers and this brother to a 3m5f chase winner is probably one for further down the line.
runners.comment: This brother to a 3m5f chase winner is probably one for further down the line

runners.name: Style De Folie
runners.spotlight: 50-1 when he finished a long way behind Testing Patience at Fontwell; cheekpieces added.
runners.comment: 50-1 when he finished a long way behind Testing Patience at Fontwell; cheekpieces added

runners.name: Scottish Anthem
runners.spotlight: Had plenty of racing on the Flat and ran to a highly respectable level last year at up to 2m; runner-up in his second run over hurdles at Uttoxeter (good) last summer and could run well if fitness doesn't hold him back after a lengthy absence.
runners.comment: Useful Flat horse who was runner-up on final run over hurdles last summer

runners.name: Virtuose d'Ainay
runners.spotlight: Had a long absence after finishing third in an Irish point and was pulled up in both runs over hurdles last year.
runners.comment: Huge odds when pulled up in both runs over hurdles last year

races.race_name: John Sillett Memorial Handicap Hurdle

runners.name: J J Moon
runners.spotlight: Only stepped up to 3m in his last three races and went close in two of those; looked as though he'd win at Newbury last time (good to soft) only to get outbattled; the cheekpieces could be significant.
runners.comment: Perhaps outbattled at Newbury last time and the cheekpieces could be significant

runners.name: Benign Dictator
runners.spotlight: Career-best form came here in December when scoring over 3m2f (good to soft); hasn't really approached that level since, in cheekpieces and blinkers (new) the last twice.
runners.comment: Course winner in December but hasn't really approached that level since

runners.name: Lipa K
runners.spotlight: Goes particularly well at Bangor but was runner-up at Aintree on final run in October (3m, good); however, a 3lb rise sees him on a career-high mark and he's been off a good while.
runners.comment: A 3lb rise for last time sees him on a career-high mark and he's been off a good while

runners.name: Everyonesgame
runners.spotlight: Below par last time at Ascot but Kempton win in November was off only 4lb lower and then ran fairly well at Cheltenham; if back to that level of form then he wouldn't be far away.
runners.comment: Below par last time but repeat of his early-season form wouldn't see him far away

runners.name: Thank You Ma'am
runners.spotlight: Back hurdling the last twice after a brief stint over fences; the first time he'd worn these aids combined when a front-running third at Wetherby (3m, good; beaten 4.25l); exposed but firmly in the mix.
runners.comment: Exposed but firmly in the mix after his return to form at Wetherby (3m)
runners.owner: The Posh Pundit Racing Club Limited

races.race_name: Sporting Agenda Challenger Series Mares' Chase Final Handicap Chase (Series Final) (GBB Race)

runners.name: Theonewedreamof
runners.spotlight: Ex-Irish mare who has been a bit up and down for Dan Skelton but did run well at Leicecster last time (2m4f, good to soft) given one notable mistake that she made; won't mind returning to further and off this mark she's entitled to have a major say.
runners.comment: Won't mind returning to further and off this mark she's entitled to have a major say

runners.name: Holetown
runners.spotlight: Dual chase winner in France and was seeing British fences for the first time when leading late on at Ludlow (2m4f, soft); unraced over this far but she wasn't stopping when a 3l third at Newbury (2m6f, good to soft) and she's back against her own sex now; good ground would be a first but provided that's suitable then should make presence felt.
runners.comment: Unraced over this far but wasn't stopping when a 3l third at Newbury

runners.name: Realta Liath
runners.spotlight: Has to bounce back from a couple of modest runs (latest second was a remote one) but she has since undergone a wind operation; closely matched with Party Vibes and Theonewedreamof on their Cheltenham clash in December; possible.
runners.comment: Closely matched with Party Vibes and Theonewedreamof on their Cheltenham clash in December

runners.name: Queens Wish
runners.spotlight: Ex-Skelton mare who is 1-8 for current yard and she's been plying her trade at a lower level; 2lb out of the weights; others much preferred.
runners.comment: 1-8 for current yard and she's been plying her trade at a lower level; 2lb wrong

runners.name: Lagonda
runners.spotlight: Blinkers are turned to after finishing tailed off a month ago and her previous thirds were no great advertisement for her prospects here; probably wants softer ground as well.
runners.comment: Others arrive here in sharper form and this mare probably wants softer ground as well

runners.name: Minniemum
runners.spotlight: 8lb higher than for last year's convincing win in this race which was staged at Cheltenham; only subsequent win in six races came at the expense of just three rivals at Ludlow in November (3m1f, good); lacklustre in her last three starts and hard to support with any confidence.
runners.comment: Won this last year but lacklustre in last three races; hard to support with any confidence

runners.name: Kosasiempre
runners.spotlight: Third to Minniemum in last year's final of this series; second run after wind surgery when winning over fences at Hereford in November and she added a 3m handicap hurdle last month at Newbury in first-time cheekpieces; solid chance off this mark.
runners.comment: Third in last year's final of this series; won over fences and hurdles this campaign

runners.name: Kalista Love
runners.spotlight: 2-5 over fences, her latest success when seeing off two other finishers at Fakenham (2m5f, soft) in February; finished behind Holetown at Newbury (2m6f) and needs to improve for this longer trip or the application of cheekpieces.
runners.comment: Probably has to improve for this longer trip or the application of cheekpieces
runners.owner: Hot To Trot Jumping And Dahlbury

runners.name: Party Vibes
runners.spotlight: Has won two qualifiers en route to this final, beating a few of these at Cheltenham in December (2m4f, good to soft) and leading late on at Hereford 17 days ago (2m5f, good); remains on a competitive mark despite all that; respected.
runners.comment: Has won two qualifiers for this final this season and yet remains on a competitive mark

runners.name: Ede'iffs Rock
runners.spotlight: 4-10 over fences and better than ever this season with front-running wins at Taunton (2m7f; good to soft/good); this track will suit her style but a collective 17lb rise for those wins could see her vulnerable.
runners.comment: In form but a collective 17lb rise for recent Taunton wins could see her vulnerable

runners.name: Sunday Soldier
runners.spotlight: Multiple hurdle winner who is 0-5 over fences and has a bit to find with some of these; she also has to rebound from a modest effort at Chepstow and her stamina isn't guaranteed.
runners.comment: Multiple hurdle winner who is 0-5 over fences and has a bit to find with some of these

races.race_name: KTDA Mares' Challenger Series Final Handicap Hurdle (Series Final) (GBB Race)

runners.name: Rula Bula
runners.spotlight: Last season started well but petered out and it might be happening again, as although it wasn't a bad run behind Cheltenham Festival heroine White Noise two starts back, Chepstow last time was underwhelming (well behind Siog Geal; soft ground no excuse).
runners.comment: Last season started well but petered out and it might be happening again

runners.name: Crazierthandaisy
runners.spotlight: Looked a bit unlucky not to win at Uttoxeter last time (2m4f, good to soft) but wouldn't be certain to run as well again given her inconsistency; 5lb above the only mark she's defied in a handicap and a 7lb claimer was employed last time.
runners.comment: 5lb above only mark she's defied in a handicap and a 7lb claimer was employed last time

runners.name: Rockola Vogue
runners.spotlight: Picked up two handicaps in the autumn (2m3f/2m7f, good) but her progress has rather stalled since then and looks on a hard mark.
runners.comment: Progress has stalled since early-season wins and looks on a hard mark

runners.name: Greyval
runners.spotlight: Won this race last year (at Cheltenham) off today's mark; turned her back on chasing this winter and ran quite well over 3m at Kempton before finding soft ground too testing at Ludlow; plenty looks in her favour this time with cheekpieces added.
runners.comment: Last year's winner; plenty in her favour for a bold defence with cheekpieces added

runners.name: Keep On Cobbling
runners.spotlight: Maiden winner who made a successful handicap debut at Sedgefield (2m5f, good) but hasn't advanced her form from there; however, soft ground perhaps not ideal the last time and this 6yo is bred to improve with her racing.
runners.comment: Soft going perhaps not ideal the last time and this 6yo is bred to improve with her racing

runners.name: Service Minimum
runners.spotlight: Would have found conditions too soft when seventh behind Pretending at Market Rasen and she then justified market confidence with a narrow win at Bangor (2m3f, good); she travelled much the best in that race but the handicapper went with an 11lb rise and that asks major questions in this loftier grade.
runners.comment: An 11lb rise for her Bangor win asks major questions in this loftier grade

runners.name: Pretending
runners.spotlight: Third to Greyval 12 months ago off the same mark; hadn't been at her best this season but came good at Market Rasen (2m4f, soft) and clearly capable of having some say in the outcome.
runners.comment: Third last year; made a handy return to form when winning at Market Rasen

runners.name: Hard Dealt
runners.spotlight: Has only won a Worcester novice and failed in six handicaps off lower marks, though was runner-up in four of those; clearly has nothing up her sleeve mark-wise but should still run well.
runners.comment: Has only won a Worcester novice and failed in six handicaps off lower marks

runners.name: Audacious Annie
runners.spotlight: First and second in her two previous visits here, latterly over this far in first-time cheekpieces in November; below best in her last two races (3m/2m4f) and appears to have little margin for error off this mark; absent since January.
runners.comment: Good record here but appears to have little margin for error off this mark

runners.name: Country Mouse
runners.spotlight: Point winner; the Ludlow third in December (2m5f novice; good to soft) offers some hope off this mark but lesser runs have followed, including her handicap debut when a 25-1 chance; cheekpieces added.
runners.comment: 3rd in a novice in December; lesser runs have followed, including handicap debut when 25-1

races.race_name: Vanessa Littler's 60th Birthday Handicap Chase

runners.name: Blackijo Dagrostis
runners.spotlight: 13-race maiden; late mistake cost him dear at Taunton two runs back (2m, good) but Hereford was disappointing when behind Ballywood; others preferred.
runners.comment: Good penultimate effort but Hereford was disappointing when behind Ballywood

runners.name: Lunar Ocean
runners.spotlight: Held up and was never that threatening when beaten just over 7l into fourth at Ffos Las (2m3f, good to soft); that's two pleasing enough runs since the visor went on and this 17-race maiden has each-way claims.
runners.comment: Two pleasing runs since the visor went on and this 17-race maiden has each-way claims

runners.name: Zoeman
runners.spotlight: Switched to fences this season and produced three good runs in defeat (2m-2m4f) before flopping in first-time cheekpieces when made favourite at Huntingdon in early February; could be given another chance with that headgear swiftly removed.
runners.comment: Three good runs over fences before flopping in first-time cheekpieces that are now removed

runners.name: Ballywood
runners.spotlight: Nearly won for the first time in seven years at Huntingdon (2m, good) but he got an easy lead then and he was a spent force some way out at Hereford; wouldn't be the percentage call.
runners.comment: Hasn't won a race for years and may need an easy lead for him to put that right

runners.name: Lone Soldier
runners.spotlight: A stumble after four out was no help at Market Rasen but did finish well held and he's now 0-9 over fences and 0-19 overall.
runners.comment: Respected on the odd bit of form but he's now 0-9 over fences and 0-19 overall

runners.name: Forcetoreckonwith
runners.spotlight: Point winner who had her consistency rewarded with a 2.25l win at Newcastle (2m, good to soft) and the runner-up has since won at Haydock; a 7lb rise looks manageable.
runners.comment: Beat a subsequent Haydock winner last time and the 7lb rise looks manageable

runners.name: Ironica De Thaix
runners.spotlight: Confined to five races since winning a French bumper in 2021; encouraging third in a maiden hurdle on stable debut but pulled up in another next time and suffered same fate on chasing/handicap debut; now has much to prove, but he's down in grade with cheekpieces added and in a good yard.
runners.comment: Pulled up on stable/handicap debut; downgraded here with cheekpieces added

runners.name: Golden Millie
runners.spotlight: Bumper winner; cut little ice over hurdles but there were positives to take from chase debut at Ffos Las (2m3f, good to soft) where she made up some ground from off the pace; still needs more and Lunar Ocean was ahead of her last time.
runners.comment: Made an okay chase debut; needs more and Lunar Ocean was ahead of her last time

runners.name: Kally Des Bruyeres
runners.spotlight: Dual hurdle winner who was having only his second run over fences when making all for a narrow success at Market Rasen (2m1f, good); up in grade off 5lb higher and there should be competition for the lead.
runners.comment: Made all last time; up in grade off 5lb higher and there could be competition for the lead

races.race_name: Molly Ollys Charity Partner Of The Year Handicap Hurdle

runners.name: Pottersjetamay
runners.spotlight: Maiden over hurdles and fences; two good efforts in small-field Fakenham chases last year but his placed form over hurdles since back from a break suggest he'll be pushed to get off the mark today.
runners.comment: Maiden hurdler/chaser; placed form since back hurdling suggest he'll do well to win today

runners.name: Major Major
runners.spotlight: Three Flat wins but no joy over hurdles so far, including in two handicaps; refitted blinkers replace cheekpieces; best watched.
runners.comment: Three Flat wins but no joy over hurdles so far, including in two handicaps; best watched

runners.name: Snapius
runners.spotlight: Best effort for a while when beating two other finishers at Huntingdon (about 2m3f, good) last month; back up 6lb in a race that will take more winning but that recent success could have had a reviving effect.
runners.comment: Best run for a while when beating 2 other finishers at Huntingdon (2m3f); this needs more

runners.name: Killycarn
runners.spotlight: Four handicap runs, winning the first of them, at Catterick (about 1m7f, good) on New Year's Day, and a good second to an improved winner at Musselburgh (2m4f, good to soft) in March; carries a big weight but respected now aiming lower.
runners.comment: Winning handicap debut in January and good 2nd to improver latest (2m4f); aiming lower now

runners.name: Ballynorth
runners.spotlight: Minor promise in Irish points and early hurdle runs but found wanting when pulled up on handicap debut at Newbury (about 2m4f, good to soft; 66-1) in March; first-time tongue-tie now replaced with a hood; downgraded today.
runners.comment: Minor promise earlier but pulled up on handicap debut at Newbury; downgraded now

runners.name: Inion Tiogair
runners.spotlight: Bumper winner in 2023 who didn't break her hurdle duck until February but she's now won three of her last four, all at Chepstow (about 2m3f; soft and good to soft); demolished her rivals last time and up a further 10lb but she's in such good form another win isn't out of the question.
runners.comment: Didn't break hurdle duck until February but now won 3 of last 4 (2m3f); up 10lb for latest

runners.name: Stung By Tariffs
runners.spotlight: Signs of some ability in early hurdle runs but he was beaten and pulled up a long way out on handicap debut at Haydock (2m5f, good to soft) last month; still early days but no rush to support him this time.
runners.comment: Minor promise in early hurdle runs but pulled up a long way out on handicap debut (2m5f)

runners.name: Orange Diamond
runners.spotlight: Three hurdle wins (2m4f; good and good to soft) in the middle of 2025, then made a winning return to fences at Market Rasen (3m) in December; not so effective on soft next time but has a  weights chance back hurdling.
runners.comment: Won 3 hurdles in 2025; winning return to fences in December; weights chance back hurdling

runners.name: Midnight View
runners.spotlight: In the frame in first two completions in handicaps (2m, good to soft and good) either side of Christmas but his Taunton run since was a distinct letdown and leaves him with plenty to prove at present.
runners.comment: In the frame in first two handicap completions but latest run was a letdown; work to do

runners.name: Son Of Hypnos
runners.spotlight: Hurdle/chase winner in Ireland but on a losing run now and, although he ran better when third of four finishers at Exeter recently than he had on his yard debut there, a few more pounds off his back wouldn't go amiss.
runners.comment: Won hurdles/chase in Ireland, latest in July 2024; more weight off back wouldn't go amiss

runners.name: Mexican Stan
runners.spotlight: Has had few chances but he called it a day on his handicap debut at Huntingdon in November and his finishing effort was sorely lacking at Sedgefield (2m1f, good to soft) in February; hard to fancy for now.
runners.comment: Had few chances but moderate efforts in two handicaps and hard to fancy for now

races.race_name: Saumur Cup Handicap Chase (GBB Race)

runners.name: Claim Du Brizais
runners.spotlight: Bumper and novice hurdle winner; 0-4 as a chaser but best form yet in this scene when a 3l fourth over C&D four weeks ago and entitled to build on that.
runners.comment: 0-4 as a chaser but best form yet in this scene when a 3l fourth over C&D four weeks ago
runners.owner: Noel Fehily Racing Syndicate - Claim Du

runners.name: Two To Tango
runners.spotlight: Mostly consistent in defeat over hurdles and the same again over fences until digging the deepest under a positive ride at Stratford (2m3f, good; first-time cheekpieces); must have every chance off 4lb higher.
runners.comment: Consistent 8yo; recent Stratford winner who should have every chance off 4lb higher

runners.name: Moonshine Man
runners.spotlight: Won over hurdles off 3lb lower; runner-up in two of his five runs over fences and was always prominent when beaten a shade over 4l over 3m at Kempton on final run in February; fine from 2m4f to 3m and his turn looks near.
runners.comment: Won over hurdles off 3lb lower; 0-5 over fences but his turn looks near

runners.name: Sleeping Satellite
runners.spotlight: Leads or races prominently; twice made all in the autumn (2m3f, good) and latest fifth at Kelso was a good run as he only weakened late on in a higher grade; respected in the new cheekpieces.
runners.comment: Latest fifth at Kelso was a good run as he only weakened late on in a higher grade

runners.name: Roger Rarebit
runners.spotlight: Back from a lengthy break to win over C&D on New Year's Eve and has continued to run respectably; consistent 9yo who should give another good account.
runners.comment: Consistent 9yo who should give another good account where he's a C&D winner

runners.name: Move With The Beat
runners.spotlight: Multiple point winner who has got his act together over fences with two wins since January; the tongue-tie was new when holding on gamely at Wincanton last time (2m4f, good); just 4lb higher.
runners.comment: The tongue-tie was new when holding on gamely at Wincanton last time; just 4lb higher.

runners.name: Caterpillar Girl
runners.spotlight: Won her bumper here; beaten between 23l and 46l over hurdles (2m) so goes chasing/handicapping with much to prove on a few counts.
runners.comment: Course bumper winner but goes chasing/handicapping with much to prove on a few counts

runners.name: Bahtiyar
runners.spotlight: Latest third here was one of his better efforts last year but that was over 3m1f in November; now 0-32 and 9lb out of the weights.
runners.comment: Off since November, he's 0-32 overall and 9lb out of the weights

races.race_name: Molly Ollys Open National Hunt Flat Race (Category 1 Elimination) (GBB Race)

runners.name: Lion Rose Sivola
runners.spotlight: Point winner who has been runner-up on both bumper starts (good to soft/heavy), most recently at Kelso in February; leading claims.
runners.comment: Point winner who has been runner-up on both hurdle starts and holds leading claims

runners.name: For A Moment
runners.spotlight: Point runner-up (3m, yielding) who also finished second on bumper debut at Ludlow (2m, good to soft) in December; better could have been expected at Huntingdon (2m, soft) next time but the Ludlow form reads well and she returns from a break with her trainer in fine form; a tongue-tie goes on.
runners.comment: Better could have been expected last time but form of previous Ludlow second reads well

runners.name: Nevada Samba
runners.spotlight: Brother to two winners who was sent off at 4-1 when winning on last month's debut at Wincanton (1m7f, good to soft), beating rivals who had already shown promise; carries a 7lb penalty against some interesting opponents today but this bright prospect is respected nevertheless.
runners.comment: 4-1 winner on debut at Wincanton, beating rivals who had already shown promise; respected

runners.name: Muscadet
runners.spotlight: Seventh foal; half-brother to three winners including The Wounded Knee (useful 2m/3m hurdle) and Reflection Of You (bumper/3m hurdle); dam bumper and 2m/2m4f hurdle winner; trainer also saddles Wincanton winner Nevada Samba; interesting to note market strength on debut.
runners.comment: Yard also runs Wincanton winner Nevada Samba; interesting to note market strength on debut

runners.name: Tommy Tipa
runners.spotlight: First foal; dam 11.6f Flat (RPR 84) and 2m/2m1f hurdle (122) winner, from useful family (both codes); trainer has had just one runner in bumpers this season and that one made a winning rules debut in September; chance.
runners.comment: Dam dual-purpose winner; trainer has had a bumper winner this season; chance on debut

runners.name: Porth Mear
runners.spotlight: Tailed off at 200-1 on last month's debut at Exeter (good to soft).
runners.comment: Tailed off at 200-1 on last month's debut at Exeter (good to soft)

runners.name: Iamtheidaho
runners.spotlight: 27,000euros 3yo; fourth foal; half-brother to winners Bareback Jack (useful 1m7f/2m hurdle) and Palpatate (bumper); dam placed in bumpers/2m hurdles, out of bumper, 2m1f Flat and useful 2m4f/2m5f hurdle winner; stable had a bumper winner at Hexham last week and he's not ruled out on debut.
runners.comment: 27,000euros 3yo; half-brother to the useful Bareback Jack; not ruled out on debut

runners.name: Jojo De Montreuil
runners.spotlight: Newcomer; 11,000euros yearling; second foal; dam French 2m1f/2m2f hurdle winner, half-sister to two French winners out of French 2m hurdle/2m3f chase winner; others make greater appeal.
runners.comment: 11,000euros yearling; dam French 2m1f/2m2f hurdle winner; others make greater appeal

runners.name: Sano City
runners.spotlight: Third foal; half-brother to 1m4f/1m6f AW (RPR 77) and 2m4f hurdle (117) winner Your Band; dam 1m4f AW winner (RPR 71), half-sister to some classy sprinters; no surprise if he gives a good account on debut.
runners.comment: Half-brother to dual-purpose performer Your Band; could give a good account on debut

runners.name: Olly's Icon
runners.spotlight: Fourth foal; dam unraced half-sister to two winners (hurdle/Flat), out of 1m4f winner; others newcomers make greater appeal on paper.
runners.comment: Fourth foal; dam unraced half-sister to two winners; other newcomers appeal more on paper

runners.name: The Drunken Sailor
runners.spotlight: Fourth foal; dam unplaced in bumper and 2m3f-3m1f hurdles, half-sister to bumper and useful 2m1f-2m2f hurdle/chase winner Watcombe Heights, out of bumper and 2m5f/2m6f hurdle winner; trainer does well in bumpers and he could go well on debut.
runners.comment: Dam from talented family; trainer does well in bumpers and he could go well on debut

runners.name: Beau Merle
runners.spotlight: Beaten 26l when fourth of eight on debut at Wincanton (1m7f, good) three weeks ago; the runner-up has since won and he's open to improvement if settling better, but others arrive with more pressing claims.
runners.comment: Open to improvement if settling better but beaten 26l when fourth on debut at Wincanton

runners.name: Rue De Lota
runners.spotlight: Related to winners in France; nibbled at in the betting when 21l eighth of ten at 28-1 on debut at Ascot (1m7f, good) in February 2025; absent since and a watching brief may be the best option on her return.
runners.comment: Down the field at Ascot in February 2025 on sole start; a watching brief may be best

runners.name: Colin's Legacy
runners.spotlight: 14-1, failed to settle and tailed off on debut at Southwell (2m, soft) last November; this half-brother to three winners is on different ground, with his trainer in fine form, but is probably still best watched.
runners.comment: 14-1, failed to settle and tailed off on debut at Southwell last November; off since

races.race_name: Stonepack Mares' 'National Hunt' Novices' Hurdle (GBB Race)

runners.name: Celtic Alliance
runners.spotlight: 7-1 when winning on debut in a 4yo maiden hurdle at Clonmel (2m, soft) three weeks ago; this half-sister to the yard's useful 2m3f/2m4f hurdle winner Absolute Notions is a bright prospect and, in conjunction with stablemate Dunsy Rock, has the best form in the field.
runners.comment: Won 4yo maiden hurdle at Clonmel three weeks ago on her sole start; major player
runners.owner: Mrs Judith Matthews & Mrs Sheila O'Ryan

runners.name: Viscountess Nelson
runners.spotlight: Point winner who built upon her bumper debut fourth when 1l runner-up at Plumpton (2m4f, good to soft) last month on her first hurdle start; she has something to find with the Gordon Elliott runners but is in top hands and open to improvement.
runners.comment: Plumpton runner-up; something to find with the Elliott runners but open to improvement
runners.owner: Old Gold Racing With Francesca Cumani

runners.name: Oor Wish
runners.spotlight: Big odds and unable to pose a threat this year on her three bumper starts (heavy-good to soft); bred to be suited by the step up in trip on hurdle debut but it still seems likely she'll come up short.
runners.comment: Bred to be suited by step up in trip on hurdle debut but still likely she'll come up short

runners.name: Frankies Fortune
runners.spotlight: Won Irish mares' maiden point (3m, yielding) on debut last April and ran okay when fifth in Carlisle bumper (2m1f, good) in October; tailed off there (2m1f, soft) the following month; should come good at some point but she's in a warm race for her hurdle debut and others are preferred.
runners.comment: Point winner; needs to improve upon her bumper form to make an impact on hurdle debut

runners.name: Quinnfinity
runners.spotlight: 6,000euros 3yo; seventh foal; half-sister to winners Lone Wolf (bumper/useful 2m2f hurdle) and Love At Sea (bumper/2m1f hurdle); dam dual point winner; she's probably best watched on debut.
runners.comment: 6,000euros 3yo; half-sister to two winners but probably best watched on debut

runners.name: Aristelle
runners.spotlight: Displayed ability when third in a bumper at Carlisle (2m1f, soft) in December but tailed off at Newcastle (2m, AW) next time and same story when 50-1 for recent hurdle debut at Carlisle (2m3f, good to soft).
runners.comment: Displayed ability in one of her bumpers but tailed off at 50-1 on recent hurdle debut

runners.name: Bracing Brook
runners.spotlight: Dam 3m hurdle winner (RPR 89); 500-1 when pulled up on debut in a novice hurdle at Ayr (2m, soft) last November; hard to fancy.
runners.comment: 500-1 when pulled up on debut in a novice hurdle at Ayr (2m, soft) last November

races.race_name: Download The Free Racing TV App Now Handicap Hurdle (Div I)

runners.name: A King Of Magic
runners.spotlight: Won in a first-time tongue-tie (retained since) at Taunton (2m, heavy) in February on second hurdle start and went close at Sedgefield (2m1f, good) last month; he's shaped as though he'll be suited by the step up in trip and there's lots to like on his handicap debut.
runners.comment: He's shaped as though he'll be suited by the step up in trip; lots to like on h'cap debut

runners.name: King Jon Oliver
runners.spotlight: Won on stable debut at Fakenham (2m4f, good to soft) in January and bounced back from heavy-ground flop on handicap debut at Hereford when fourth at Uttoxeter (2m7f, good to soft) last month; shaped last time as though this shorter trip would help and trainer has a good record in this race.
runners.comment: The drop back down in trip could help and his trainer has a good record in this race

runners.name: Joeybottles
runners.spotlight: Ran well in a bumper here last April on debut (good to soft) and, having had wind surgery, he made a winning handicap debut at 9-2 at Bangor (2m3f, good) last month; did it easily and a 10lb rise may not stop him.
runners.comment: Easy win on last month's handicap debut at Bangor and a 10lb rise may not stop him

runners.name: Platin Moon
runners.spotlight: Front-runner who won at Ayr (2m, heavy) in January before solid third at Musselburgh (1m7f, good to soft), and he won again at Ayr (2m, soft) last month; further improvement is necessary today, but possible.
runners.comment: Front-runner who has won two of his last three starts; further improvement is possible

runners.name: Bois Guillbert
runners.spotlight: Three-time hurdle winner who has dropped to a mark 5lb lower than when winning on his chase debut last February; he may stage a revival at some stage but has struggled over fences/hurdles on his three runs this season.
runners.comment: On a dangerous mark but has struggled over fences/hurdles on his three runs this season

runners.name: Largy Meadow
runners.spotlight: Improved form when 16-1 runner-up at Newcastle (2m4f, good) 12 days ago on second handicap start; needs to back up that latest performance but he's unexposed on good ground and drying conditions would aid his cause.
runners.comment: Went close at Newcastle recently and drying conditions would aid his cause

runners.name: La Vish
runners.spotlight: He was the 20-1 outsider of three when winning a novice at Warwick (2m5f, good) four weeks ago on his third hurdle start; this 5yo handicap newcomer is unexposed but the strength of that latest form is questionable given the heavily odds-on favourite severely underperformed.
runners.comment: Outsider of three when winning Warwick novice; the strength of that form is questionable

runners.name: Monaco Rules
runners.spotlight: French hurdle winner who is 0-7 in Britain but he went close at Sedgefield (2m4f, soft) last November when last seen; also effective on good to soft ground; could be in the mix if fully tuned up after a break.
runners.comment: French hurdle winner who went close at Sedgefield in November; this is his first run since

runners.name: Bel Ombre
runners.spotlight: Well backed into 85-40 when winning by 3l on handicap debut at Taunton (2m3f, good to soft) in January; 10lb higher for this first run since but the form has worked out well and he could have plenty more left in the tank.
runners.comment: Well-backed winner on handicap debut at Taunton in January & the form has worked out well

races.race_name: Download The Free Racing TV App Now Handicap Hurdle (Div II)

runners.name: Karton Plein
runners.spotlight: Won a maiden hurdle at Southwell (2m, good to soft) last February and he returned to form when third at Lingfield (2m3f, soft) in January; pulled up at Southwell (2m4f, good) since but not written off for a trainer who has a good recent record in this race; one of two for the yard.
runners.comment: Third at Lingfield in January; pulled up at Southwell since but he's not written off

runners.name: Lets Mingle
runners.spotlight: Won C&D maiden (good) last May and runner-up on handicap debut at Kelso (2m6f, good) in October; below par on her next two runs but one was over 3m and the latest was on heavy going; this trip on drying ground could prove ideal and she's a possible contender.
runners.comment: Below par on her last two starts but lightly raced and today's conditions could help

runners.name: Soldier's Leap
runners.spotlight: Won two in a row in the autumn (2m2f/2m, good to soft/soft) and, after a couple of respectable fifth placings, he was a close second at Stratford (2m2f, good) last month; in the mix if seeing out this longer trip; the hood worn on his last four starts is left off.
runners.comment: 1l second at Stratford last month and he can be in the mix if seeing out this longer trip

runners.name: Buy Some Time
runners.spotlight: Soundly beaten at Kelso three weeks ago but may have needed that first run since last July; won a big-field handicap at 50-1 at Punchestown (2m3f, yielding) last April and is on a handy mark on that form; not ruled out.
runners.comment: Soundly beaten at Kelso recently but may have needed it; handy mark on last April's form

runners.name: Stoner's Choice
runners.spotlight: Course winner whose mark continues to fall and he's not been devoid of spark this season; however, this 11yo is without a win since April 2023 and was disappointing at Ludlow last month.
runners.comment: Course winner whose mark continues to fall but this 11yo is winless since April 2023

runners.name: Gentleman Bill
runners.spotlight: Promising over hurdles last season and he's well handicapped on his chase form from the end of last year; however, he's finished down the field over fences/hurdles on his last two starts; blinkers replace cheekpieces.
runners.comment: Well handicapped on this season's chase form but down the field on his last two starts

runners.name: Heard That
runners.spotlight: Won off 7lb higher over fences here (3m, good to soft) last April; 0-9 over hurdles but runner-up in this discipline at Southwell (2m4f, good) last month; one of two for trainer with a good record in the race; chance.
runners.comment: Won off 7lb higher over fences here last April; 2nd over hurdles at Southwell last month

runners.name: Letterston Lily
runners.spotlight: This is her first run since November 2024 and she needs to prove how much ability she retains; however, this lightly raced 6yo is on a competitive judged on her handicap form in autumn 2024 and it will be interesting to see how she goes in the betting on her return to action.
runners.comment: Absent since November 2024 but on a competitive mark and one to watch in the betting

runners.name: Timefortom
runners.spotlight: Point winner who was runner-up this season on his first two hurdle starts (2m5f/2m4f, good/soft); pulled up at Newcastle (2m4f, good to soft) in January but has had a break since and he has potential off his opening mark if tapping back into his earlier promise.
runners.comment: Pulled up latest but has potential off opening mark if tapping back into earlier promise

races.race_name: River Tay Handicap Chase (GBB Race)

runners.name: Toothless
runners.spotlight: Won at Windsor (2m4f, good) on New Year's Day on second start back from an absence and runner-up to progressive winners on next two starts (2m4f, soft); could still have more to offer himself and he's high on the list.
runners.comment: Won at Windsor in January and runner-up to progressive winners on next two starts

runners.name: Inox Allen
runners.spotlight: Easily made all in first-time visor over C&D (good to soft) last April; hard to fancy on his three runs this term but, given his previous reaction to new headgear, it's possible cheekpieces will garner a positive response.
runners.comment: C&D winner; hard to fancy on his 3 runs this term but change of headgear could be a plus

runners.name: Westerninthepark
runners.spotlight: Disappointing on his last few starts and a major turnaround is necessary; however, he's dropped to a dangerous mark and his trainer has an excellent strike-rate at Perth; no surprise if he bounces back.
runners.comment: Major turnaround needed but he's on a dangerous mark and trainer has excellent record here

runners.name: Slanagaibhgoleir
runners.spotlight: Won at Down Royal (2m4f, good to yielding) in December and ran well in defeat on next two starts (2m3f/2m4f, heavy/good to yielding) prior to failing to stay 3m4f in the Ulster National at Downpatrick; could play a leading role back down in trip.
runners.comment: Some good form in this first chasing campaign; didn't stay 3m4f in Ulster National latest

runners.name: Whistleinthedark
runners.spotlight: 11yo who was tailed off at Cheltenham in December and pulled up at Haydock this month on sole run since; arrives with something to prove but three visits to Perth have yielded two wins, including this race in 2023, so he's worth a second look; a first-time visor replaces cheekpieces.
runners.comment: He's struggled on his last two starts but three visits to Perth have yielded two wins

runners.name: Classic Maestro
runners.spotlight: Good second at Sandown (1m7f, soft) in January and ran well before fading into 14 third at Ayr (2m4f, soft) last month; however, his best form has come on soft/heavy ground and such conditions seem unlikely today.
runners.comment: Some positive runs this term but he may not have his favoured soft/heavy ground

runners.name: Tommy's Oscar
runners.spotlight: 11yo who snapped a losing sequence at Carlisle (3m, good to soft) last month and it was a stronger race than this in which he was fourth of 11 at Haydock (2m4f, good) last Wednesday; every chance he'll be in the mix.
runners.comment: Won at Carlisle then fourth in stronger race than this at Haydock last week; in the mix

races.race_name: Join Racing TV Now Handicap Chase (GBB Race)

runners.name: Mountain Mike
runners.spotlight: Won a maiden hurdle at Ffos Las in February 2025 (2m6f, heavy); two fair runs over fences this term (fell at the fourth last time) but taking on stronger company here.
runners.comment: Maiden hurdle winner who has run two fair races over fences this term; stiffer task here

runners.name: If Not For Dylan
runners.spotlight: Disappointing at Haydock last time (3m1f, good) but successful at Doncaster before that (3m, good to soft); each-way chance.
runners.comment: Disappointing last time but Doncaster winner before that and has an each-way chance

runners.name: Minella Drama
runners.spotlight: Very useful chaser at his best, last win coming in the 2024 Old Roan Chase at Aintree (2m4f, good); highly tried this term, best run when second in a Listed chase at Kelso in February (2m7f, good to soft); on a fair mark now.
runners.comment: Very useful at best; good second over 2m7f at Kelso in February; on a good mark now

runners.name: American Mike
runners.spotlight: Smart novice over fences for Gordon Elliott; not quite as good these days but fair third at Newbury in November (2m4f, good to soft) on latest chase start; now 5lb lower and, following a decent run over hurdles last time, is worth considering.
runners.comment: Smart novice for Gordon Elliott; not as good now but on a fair mark; interesting

runners.name: Ned Tanner
runners.spotlight: Dual winner over 2m4f on soft ground last season; has been rather inconsistent since wind surgery this term; has won over this trip, but perhaps better at shorter and has no easy task from 3lb out of the handicap.
runners.comment: Has won over this trip but perhaps better at shorter; inconsistent this term; 3lb wrong

runners.name: Statuario
runners.spotlight: Six-time course winner who was runner-up in this race last year (good to soft); has since gained two C&D wins on good ground, latest in September; has plenty going for him; leading contender.
runners.comment: Second in this race in 2025; two C&D wins on good since, latest in September; contender

runners.name: Della Casa Lunga
runners.spotlight: Mainly in fair form this season and can be forgiven a below-par run on testing ground at Exeter last time; these conditions suit; each-way chance with cheekpieces back on.
runners.comment: Mainly in fair form this season (below par on heavy latest) and has an each-way chance

races.race_name: Scone International Horse Trials Handicap Hurdle

runners.name: Fouroneohfever
runners.spotlight: Useful handicapper on the Flat for George Boughey; off the mark on fourth hurdle start when taking Ludlow handicap in good style last time; drying ground suits; should go well despite 9lb rise.
runners.comment: Useful on the Flat; won handicap hurdle latest; drier going suits; chance despite 9lb rise

runners.name: Mister Ursus
runners.spotlight: Confirmed previous promise over hurdles when an easy winner of a maiden at Fakenham last time; stiffer task here but looks the stable's number one and he's a possible player.
runners.comment: Won maiden hurdle last time; stiffer task here but looks stable's first choice; chance

runners.name: Someone's Wish
runners.spotlight: Dual AW Flat winner who has won a chase and a handicap hurdle at Downpatrick since joining Gordon Elliott; hasn't been at his best since winning over hurdles in October (2m1f, yielding); stable has a fine record here.
runners.comment: AW Flat/hurdle/chase winner; not at best recently; needs to return to form but a possible
runners.owner: Call It What You Like Syndicate

runners.name: The Hatchet
runners.spotlight: Winner of a C&D novice hurdle in September (good to soft); mainly fair form since, although only third of four at 1-2 in a Newcastle handicap last time (2m1f, good to soft); tongue-tie now tried; each-way chance.
runners.comment: C&D novice win in September; some fair form since (disappointing latest); each-way chance

runners.name: Adonedeal
runners.spotlight: Irish point winner in May 2025 (3m, good to firm); won first two starts over hurdles, but a bit to prove after a poor run on handicap debut last time at Stratford.
runners.comment: Irish point winner (good to firm); won first two races over hurdles but below par latest

runners.name: Magnolia
runners.spotlight: Runner-up in the 2m4f mares' handicap here last April when rated 13lb lower; won twice over 2m at Kelso in the autumn (good/soft) but hasn't done as well since; bit to prove now.
runners.comment: Second over 2m4f here last April; two 2m Kelso wins since, but out of form recently

runners.name: Just Dottie
runners.spotlight: Easy winner off a 10lb lower mark after a break at this meeting last year (fifth over 2m here in April 2024); won another race over C&D off a pound lower than here in May (good) and also on the Flat at Ayr in September (soft); interesting.
runners.comment: Easy winner after a break at this fixture in 2025; also won over C&D in May; interesting

runners.name: Pure Gold
runners.spotlight: Has won three races this season, the second over this C&D in September; up in the ratings and off since November; may be better for the run.
runners.comment: Three wins this term, including over C&D; back after a break; may be better for the run
runners.owner: Thp Racing & D A Harrison Racing

runners.name: Scriabin
runners.spotlight: Ex-Irish; 11-race maiden over hurdles; placed in two of his three runs for current yard, last time finishing runner-up to a subsequent easy winner in a maiden hurdle at Musselburgh (2m1f, good to soft); interesting.
runners.comment: Ex-Irish; 0-11; second on third run for this yard at Musselburgh last time; interesting

runners.name: Winds Of Winter
runners.spotlight: Progressed well after winning a Cartmel seller last May (2m1f, good to soft) with two wins and two seconds, one of them over this C&D, and went up 25lb; hasn't been out since being pulled up at Hexham in October; interesting if fully wound up after her winter break.
runners.comment: Showed improved form after Cartmel selling win last May; off since October; could go well

runners.name: How About No
runners.spotlight: Won a Catterick novice hurdle in January 2025 (1m7f, soft); was then off for 305 days and hasn't run quite as well since; cheekpieces now tried.
runners.comment: Novice hurdle winner in 2025; then off 305 days; not fared as well since; headgear tried

runners.name: A Different Kind
runners.spotlight: Hasn't won since scoring over fences here in September 2023 (2m4f, soft; 2-7 favourite); decent run after a wind op when second to a subsequent winner in a 2m handicap hurdle at Doncaster last time; chance on that form.
runners.comment: No win since chase success here in 2023; good 2nd over hurdles latest; chance on that form

races.race_name: One Place. Every Race. Racing TV Handicap Hurdle

runners.name: Bunker Bay
runners.spotlight: 0-8 over hurdles but has posted some good performances this season, most recently runner-up at Market Rasen (2m, good) 18 days ago; also effective on soft ground; makes a lot more appeal than most of these.
runners.comment: 0-8 over hurdles but some good runs this term; makes a lot more appeal than most of these

runners.name: Game Beaaa
runners.spotlight: Patchy form last August-October and this is her first run since; however, she may be better around this time of the year than she is in the autumn and she won after a similar absence last June; not ruled out.
runners.comment: Off since last October but she won after a similar absence last June; not ruled out

runners.name: Beat The Retreat
runners.spotlight: Inconsistent but won at Ayr (2m3f, heavy) in December and returned to form to win at Musselburgh (2m4f, soft) in February on his latest start; ran well on good ground here last May and it's possible he'll cope with the drop back in trip; he can be in the shake-up if on another going day.
runners.comment: Inconsistent but has won two of his last three; in the shake-up if again on a going day

runners.name: Canavan
runners.spotlight: 4yo who has shown ability on the Flat and it's still very early days over hurdles; however, he was soundly beaten on his handicap hurdle debut at Musselburgh (1m7f, good to soft) in February and needs to raise his game.
runners.comment: Still early days over hurdles but soundly beaten on h'cap debut and has to raise his game

runners.name: Defying Gravity
runners.spotlight: Made his handicap debut at Newcastle (2m1f, good to soft) last month following wind surgery and a break and ran pretty well in fifth of 13, beaten 8l; races off the same mark today and could be an each-way contender.
runners.comment: Ran pretty well when fifth of 13 on recent handicap debut; could be an each-way contender

runners.name: Magic Gloves
runners.spotlight: Tailed off at Musselburgh in February when last seen and hard to fancy on the back of that; however, two starts ago she ran well in fourth on her handicap debut at Musselburgh (1m7f, soft) and this very lightly raced point winner is not written off.
runners.comment: Tailed off in February but encouraging handicap debut prior to that; not written off

runners.name: Sao Carlos
runners.spotlight: Well handicapped on form as recent as last February for Olly Murphy but he's struggled this season and hopes are pinned on the wind operation prompting a dramatic revival.
runners.comment: He's struggled this season; hopes are pinned on the wind op prompting a dramatic revival
runners.owner: A Balmer And Bolton Mill Racing

runners.name: Justus
runners.spotlight: Four-time Flat winner who landed a novice hurdle in January 2024; his fifth of 11 at Wetherby (2m, good to soft) last month was a step back in the right direction but he needs another today.
runners.comment: Step back in right direction at Wetherby last month but he needs another today

runners.name: Storm The Dug
runners.spotlight: 4yo who is yet to threaten across his three hurdle starts but last time was better and he has potential off his opening jumps mark based on the ability he has shown on the Flat; could be involved.
runners.comment: 4yo who has potential off opening hurdle mark based on the ability shown on the Flat

runners.name: Shadowfax Of Rohan
runners.spotlight: Won on stable/chase debut at Catterick (1m7f, good to soft) in February before bumping into progressive winner there (1m7f, good), and returned to winning ways at Southwell (2m, good) last month; 0-7 over hurdles but could be a very different proposition now; has same mark as last time.
runners.comment: 2-3 over fences for new yard; runs off his last winning mark returned to hurdles
runners.owner: K Jardine R Griffiths W Duffus

runners.name: Breadalbane Lass
runners.spotlight: Remains a maiden after 12 starts but she ran well when fourth at Carlisle (2m1f, good to soft) last month and could get closer to the target today if staying straight (hung badly left on the run-in last time).
runners.comment: 0-12 but ran well when fourth at Carlisle last month, despite hanging badly left late on

runners.name: Gone With The Tide
runners.spotlight: Ran a couple of reasonable races towards the end of last season and he's dropped down the weights; however, he's been pulled up on all three starts this term; transformation needed in first-time cheekpieces.
runners.comment: Down in the weights but pulled up on all three starts this season; cheekpieces go on

runners.name: Tigress Lilly
runners.spotlight: Not obviously well treated off this opening mark judged on her three hurdle runs last autumn; however, she's in good hands and wears a first-time tongue-tie, and things may click at some point.
runners.comment: Not obviously well treated off this opening mark but things may click at some point

runners.name: Malangen
runners.spotlight: 11yo who struggled on his last three starts last autumn; however, he won off this mark at Market Rasen (2m4f, good) last July and is a four-time winner at Perth; not ruled out on his return to action.
runners.comment: 11yo; struggled on last three starts last autumn but has won here 4 times; not ruled out
runners.owner: T Hunter & D A Harrison Racing

runners.name: Bray Dale
runners.spotlight: Third on handicap debut at Ayr (2m, soft) last March but this season has been a struggle; 3lb wrong today; visor replaces cheekpieces.
runners.comment: Third on handicap debut at Ayr (2m, soft) last March but this season has been a struggle

runners.name: Breaking Ground
runners.spotlight: She hasn't achieved much in her short career but showed some ability when fifth in a maiden hurdle at Musselburgh (2m1f, good to soft) last month, and makes her handicap debut off a lowly mark; not discounted.
runners.comment: Showed some ability at Musselburgh last month and makes handicap debut off a lowly mark

races.race_name: Every Race Live On Racing TV Conditional Jockeys' Handicap Chase (GBB Race)

runners.name: Jirko
runners.spotlight: 11-race maiden; placed on all five starts over fences this term, although was disappointing when last of three with cheekpieces tried at Ayr last time (2m4f, soft; didn't jump well); headgear retained; each-way chance.
runners.comment: 0-11; placed all 5 starts chasing (last of three in cheekpieces latest); each-way chance

runners.name: Lady In The Park
runners.spotlight: Won this race in first-time blinkers off a pound lower mark 12 months ago (good to soft); hasn't run quite as well in three starts this season and now tried in a visor; a possible.
runners.comment: Won this race in 2025; not run quite as well since; a possible with new headgear tried

runners.name: Inis Oirr
runners.spotlight: Both wins have been at Musselburgh, latest a 3m7f handicap chase in February 2024 (good to soft); struggled to win since, but is well handicapped.
runners.comment: Only chase win was over 3m7f in February 2024; struggled to win since; on a good mark now

runners.name: Burgundy Man
runners.spotlight: Made a winning debut over fences over C&D in October (soft); bit disappointing when returning from a break when well beaten at Kelso last time (2m5f, good) but should come on for the run.
runners.comment: C&D winner on chase debut in September; may have needed the run after a break last time

runners.name: Sir Carnegie
runners.spotlight: Won novice handicaps over this trip at Newcastle (good) and Kelso (good to soft) earlier in the season; not in quite such good form since but excuses for heavy defeat at Newcastle last time (bad mistake five out); not out of it.
runners.comment: Two novice h'caps wins earlier in the season; excuse for poor run last time; not out of it

runners.name: Casual Observer
runners.spotlight: Winning hurdler (2m4f, heavy) who has shown promise in defeat over 2m4f on his two chase starts (soft); now steps up to 3m for first time under rules (was second in a 3m point).
runners.comment: Winning hurdler (2m4f) who has shown promise on both chase starts; first rules run at 3m
runners.owner: Noel Fehily Racing Syndicate - Casual Ob

runners.name: Uptown Harry
runners.spotlight: Veteran; C&D winner in 2021; having a good season, winning at Newcastle in January (2m7f, good to soft) and in the frame on his three runs since, last time when 9lb wrong; runs off his true mark here; could go well.
runners.comment: Newcastle win in January; in the frame since, latest from 9lb out the h'cap; could go well

runners.name: Jakana
runners.spotlight: Won twice over hurdles for Fergal O'Brien last season; has fared less well for new yard and, having been pulled up wearing headgear on last three starts, has plenty to prove on her chase debut (no headgear this time).
runners.comment: Ex-Fergal O'Brien last term; below par this season; plenty to prove on her chase debut

races.race_name: Book Attraction Restaurant Online Restricted Maiden Stakes (Bands B, C And D) (GBB Race)

runners.name: Al Maslool
runners.spotlight: Thrice-raced colt who made no impression at Yarmouth (kept to 1m) on turf debut most recently but may be ready for a crack at this longer distance; one of the main contenders on penultimate effort, namely 1l third at Lingfield (AW).
runners.comment: One of the main contenders on penultimate effort; ready for a crack at this new trip

runners.name: Cape Fear
runners.spotlight: Seemed in need of the experience and/or found 1m on the sharp side in sole 2yo start; fared better with third-place finish upped to 1m2f at Bath (good) on reappearance and is bred to do better still, being related to a few winners for his yard; respected.
runners.comment: Fared better upped to 1m2f at Bath; related to a few winners for his yard; respected

runners.name: Fighter's Spirit
runners.spotlight: Godolphin debutant by Masar out of French 7f-winning (RPR 80) half-sister to Listed scorers Promesse De L'Aube (German 1m2f), Elite Army (1m4f) and Pure Diamond (UAE 7f); heed the market signals.
runners.comment: Godolphin debutant by Masar out of 7f winner; heed the market signals

runners.name: Three No Trumps
runners.spotlight: Belied his 100-1 odds with close third at Kempton (1m, AW) in sole 2yo run; posted a lesser effort when 25-1 at Doncaster (1m2f) on reappearance but may improve with the outing under his belt and this seems a slightly easier race.
runners.comment: May improve with Doncaster reappearance under his belt and this seems an easier race

runners.name: It's Debatable
runners.spotlight: 40,000gns yearling; brother to French 1m/1m4f winner Dixmude; dam US 8.5f turf scorer, half-sister to winners Vue Fantastique (1m2f Group 3) and Calling Out (1m 2yo Listed); this debutant represents an in-form stable; interesting.
runners.comment: 40,000gns yearling; this debutant represents an in-form stable; interesting

runners.name: Barak Warrior
runners.spotlight: 30,000euros yearling, 20,000gns 2yo; by Teofilo and first foal of twice-raced (RPR 62) daughter of Irish 1,000 Guineas winner; one of several newcomers in the field; market instructive.
runners.comment: 20,000gns 2yo; by Teofilo; one of several newcomers in the field; market instructive
runners.owner: W Sweatman, J Summers, R Foden

runners.name: Le Morbihan
runners.spotlight: 65,000euros yearling; by Sea The Moon; dam unraced sister to 7f/8.6f 2yo winner Pomelo, half-sister to Group 1 scorers Bluestocking (1m2f/1m4f including Arc) and Qirat (1m Sussex Stakes); nicely bred newcomer.
runners.comment: 65,000euros yearling; from family of Group 1 scorers; nicely bred newcomer

runners.name: Mayflower Billy
runners.spotlight: Outsider in both starts; appeared to run well, finishing fourth, at Thirsk (1m) switched to turf on reappearance but looks the type who'll find better opportunities in handicaps shortly.
runners.comment: Appeared to run well at Thirsk last time but looks one for handicaps

races.race_name: Racing To School Reaches 25 Years Restricted Novice Stakes (Bands B, C & D) (GBB/IRE Incentive Race)

runners.name: Lauralynn
runners.spotlight: Suffered a wide passage on C&D debut (good to soft; 40-1) last week where she shaped quite nicely, losing second only late on behind a smart newcomer; should improve but there are dangers among the newcomers.
runners.comment: Quite promising debut 3rd over C&D (40-1) last week; open to improvement
runners.owner: Ger O'Neill & Unique Retreats Stays Ltd

runners.name: Matteo
runners.spotlight: February foal; 43,000euros yearling by Coulsty; fourth foal; half-brother to 6f AW winner Alainn Tu (RPR 58); dam 5f 2yo winner (90), half-sister to Listed-placed 7f/1m winner Sensei; yard has already unearthed a good winning 2yo newcomer this year (favourite) and won this with a beginner last April; likely type.
runners.comment: Out of 5f winner; yard won this last year and already had a winning 2yo newcomer in 2026

runners.name: Concert Pitch
runners.spotlight: February foal; 7,000euros yearling; sixth foal; by Kuroshio; half-brother to winners Philos (1m4f/1m6f AW; RPR 67) and Cham's Pride (Spanish 9.5f including 2yo); dam French 9.5f turf/10.7f AW winner; looks best watched making debut against 2yos with speedier pedigrees.
runners.comment: 7,000euros yearling; likely best watched against 2yos with speedier pedigrees

runners.name: Craven Force
runners.spotlight: February foal; 16,000gns yearling; sixth foal; by Caturra; half-brother to winners Make Us Proud (1m4f/1m7f including AW/Australia; RPR 95) and Sesame Birah (Listed-placed 7f 2yo; 93); dam 1m4f AW winner (79); no surprise if this 5f proves on the sharp side.
runners.comment: 16,000gns yearling; no surprise if this 5f proves on the sharp side on debut
runners.owner: Craven Surface , Mrs T Burns & Partners

runners.name: Beautiful Rainbow
runners.spotlight: April foal; 40,000euros yearling; first foal; by Cotai Glory out of 6f 2yo/1m winner (RPR 83) whose winning siblings include Amadeus Wolf (6f 2yo Group 1) and Rouleau (6f 2yo Group 3); bred to go a bit and yard 1-7 with 2yos here in last five years; market check needed.
runners.comment: Useful sprinting pedigree; yard 1-7 with 2yos here in last five years; market check needed

runners.name: Disbelief
runners.spotlight: January foal; 11,000gns foal, £26,000 yearling; first foal; by Ubettabelieveit out of 7f/1m maiden (RPR 41), half-sister to winners All Star Cast (French Listed-placed 7f 2yo) and Manuha (1m); has been gelded; yard had a shortish-priced beginner run respectably this month; market can guide.
runners.comment: Early foal; £26,000 yearling; has been gelded; market can guide

runners.name: Ronson
runners.spotlight: February foal; second foal; by Ubettabelieveit out of 6f winner (RPR 57), half-sister to several winners including Bahuta Acha (5f/6f including 2yo; RPR 84) and Mininggold (5f/6f including 2yo; 82); yard has low strike-rate in 2yo maiden and novice events in recent years; likely other days.
runners.comment: Useful sprinting pedigree; yard has low strike-rate in 2yo maiden/novices in recent years

runners.name: Arrbob
runners.spotlight: Dam had some speedy siblings, including Group 3 winner Strath Burn; 20-1 for debut in the Brocklesby at Doncaster (5f, good to soft) in March where he was very slowly away but showed some minor promise thereafter; this stiffer test may well suit and can expect improvement.
runners.comment: Bred to be a good sprinter; very slowly away but some promise on debut; can improve

runners.name: Amantha
runners.spotlight: 28-1 for her recent debut over C&D when slowly away and finished at the back of the pack; unlikely for now; yard also runs Concert Pitch.
runners.comment: 28-1 for recent C&D debut when finishing at the back of the pack; unlikely this time

races.race_name: Happy Birthday Andy Hill Handicap

runners.name: I'm Next
runners.spotlight: Has won three of 11 handicaps (heavy/AW/good to soft; acts on good to firm), also runner-up to strong candidates on two occasions, including when losing his lead near the line over C&D last week; solid chance off the same mark; due 1lb rise.
runners.comment: 3-11 in handicaps; decent reappearance 2nd over C&D last week; due 1lb rise

runners.name: Tees Aggregates
runners.spotlight: Can be slowly away but he showed no mercy to his rivals when making all over C&D (good to firm) last August; returned from a long winter break with an unlucky run on Southwell AW 16 days ago and he's one to be interested in back on this track; yard won race in 2022.
runners.comment: Powerful front-running win over C&D in August; unlucky in running on recent AW return
runners.owner: Ingleby Bloodstock Ltd & The Ivy League

runners.name: Trilby
runners.spotlight: Three of five wins have come at Haydock but he found an impressive finish when winning over C&D (good) last April on sole course start; on his lowest mark since the summer of 2024 and can go well with tongue-tie refitted.
runners.comment: Impressive C&D winner last April on sole course start; handily weighted and can go well

runners.name: Novello Lad
runners.spotlight: All best form at 6f on good or quicker ground; easily excused a modest farewell to 2025 on account of soft going but he does have something to prove in his first-ever 5f run.
runners.comment: All best form at 6f on good or quicker; lacks recent run and now has first-ever run at 5f

runners.name: Fuji Mountain
runners.spotlight: Two 5f wins at Chester (good to soft and soft) as 2yo; had a tough 3yo season as a result but there were signs of life off a reduced mark in the autumn and he only just lost out at Pontefract (5f, good; first-time blinkers) on Tuesday; bang there if turning out again.
runners.comment: Two 5f wins as 2yo; tough in 2025 but only just touched off at Pontefract on Tuesday

runners.name: Until Dawn
runners.spotlight: Two 5f wins on the AW early in 2025 but she was well below her best on sand in the autumn; has had wind surgery since but the suitability of this track is in doubt now back on turf for the first time in nearly a year.
runners.comment: Two AW wins at 5f in early 2025; below par in the autumn; first turf run for nearly a year
runners.owner: F Reay, S McMillan & S Pedersen

runners.name: Jenever
runners.spotlight: Unraced on this course but some of his best form has come over Sandown's similarly stiff 5f and he's back on the same mark as his latest win there last June (good; a race he'd won 12 months earlier); every chance of being seen to good effect back on turf.
runners.comment: Unraced here but this stiff 5f can suit and he's back on same mark as latest Sandown win

runners.name: Arctic Summer
runners.spotlight: Won over 6f in France (very soft) as 2yo; couldn't cope with high marks in Britain last year and made no serious impact on reappearance at Doncaster (6f, good to soft) last month; may need more weight off her back.
runners.comment: Won over 6f in France as 2yo; high in weights since; may need more weight off her back

runners.name: Ventura Express
runners.spotlight: Has won two of three C&D starts (good and good to firm), the latest in July 2024; went very close to breaking the losing run when involved in a tight finish on Southwell AW in March; convincing claims.
runners.comment: Won two of three C&D starts; on losing run but went close on AW latest; solid claims

races.race_name: Alan McGuinness And Robin Lunness Memorial Handicap

runners.name: Milteye
runners.spotlight: Campaigned mostly on good ground or softer; tactically versatile; overcame traffic issues to get up in the final strides in apprentice race at Ripon (1m) last week, taking turf record to 5-20; drawn wide but otherwise has strong claims with penalty avoided; future mark 3lb higher.
runners.comment: Escapes penalty for Ripon win last week; drawn wide but otherwise holds strong claims
runners.owner: Rise One Racing And W A Bethell

runners.name: A War Eagle
runners.spotlight: Low-mileage 4yo whose last two efforts, latest on stable/seasonal debut, can be excused; steadily progressive RPRs otherwise and gained sole success over C&D (good to firm); interesting back down in class and returned to this scenario.
runners.comment: Last two efforts can be excused; steadily progressive otherwise; gained sole win over C&D

runners.name: Frostmagic
runners.spotlight: Has won over middle distances, including on turf, but seems to need shorter nowadays and made all over 1m at Southwell (AW) when last seen; could make a bold bid on this track provided he again goes from the front; stable going very well (3-5 with big LSP) this month; respected.
runners.comment: Made all at Southwell when last seen; could make another bold bid; stable 3-5 this month

runners.name: Highfield Viking
runners.spotlight: Gained last year's two wins in August but has recorded his other two successes (2023/2024) in the month of April; drawn against the inner rail and could be a danger, provided he's in top form on seasonal debut; market support would heighten interest.
runners.comment: Drawn against the inner rail and could be a danger provided he's in top form

runners.name: Yaajooz
runners.spotlight: Gained sole win in July 2024 on final start for previous yard; record of 0-9 for current stable doesn't tell the whole story as he's posted a few second-place finishes that represent peak form; capable of going well.
runners.comment: Form for current yard features a few second-place finishes that represent peak form

runners.name: Nyman
runners.spotlight: Ex-Irish 4yo whose form (three attempts) for new yard features a win at Newmarket (7f, good to soft) last backend; possibilities returned to turf with AW reappearance (midfield) under his belt.
runners.comment: Won at Newmarket in most recent turf attempt; possibilities back in this sphere

runners.name: Surprised
runners.spotlight: Lightly raced 4yo whose solid record in novice events, including 2yo success over C&D, has not been transferred to two handicaps; sold out of Ed Bethell's yard for £6,000 since last run; return to this scenario may have positive effect on stable/seasonal debut.
runners.comment: Lightly raced; return to this C&D may have positive effect on stable/seasonal debut

runners.name: Tropez Power
runners.spotlight: Gained sole turf success in 2021 and it's two years since last AW win; 0-4 for current stable and lacks recent match practice having been absent since November; probably best watched unless the market suggests otherwise.
runners.comment: On a two-year losing spell; 0-4 for current stable; off since November

runners.name: Capital Guarantee
runners.spotlight: Has failed to show his form in last four appearances but ground softer than good wasn't ideal on seasonal debut latest; 2lb below last winning mark and holds a fighting chance provided he rebounds.
runners.comment: 2lb below last winning mark; fighting chance provided he rebounds

runners.name: Lord Roxby
runners.spotlight: Campaigned mostly over sprint trips and gained his Beverley win at 5f (also effective at 6f); close second at Newmarket when last seen but doesn't appear to be crying out for this step back up in distance.
runners.comment: Ran well when last seen but isn't crying out for this step back up in distance
runners.owner: N Skinner D Jenkins J Glover

runners.name: Priapos
runners.spotlight: Proved suited by the switch to AW towards the end of his 2025 campaign and nearly completed a 1m hat-trick on final outing; chance depends on whether he can keep up the good form returned to turf; only 1-12 in this sphere and 10lb higher than last attempt.
runners.comment: Chance depends on whether he can transfer good AW form back to turf; 1-12 in this sphere

races.race_name: Rapid Lad Handicap

runners.name: Kitsune Power
runners.spotlight: Defied a much higher mark at Beverley in 2022; poor strike-rate since but he ran encouragingly when favourite at Catterick (1m4f) on seasonal debut and has possibilities back here, being so nicely weighted.
runners.comment: Ran encouragingly on seasonal debut; defied a much higher mark at Beverley in 2022

runners.name: Iwantmytimewithyou
runners.spotlight: Low-mileage 4yo who completed a turf double (1m4f/1m6f) last July; creditable second at Kempton (1m4f, AW) on reappearance; has shown all that best form in Class 6 but may do better still this term; respected.
runners.comment: Completed a turf double last July; ran creditably on AW reappearance; respected

runners.name: Freddy Robinson
runners.spotlight: Gained most recent win in handicap hurdle in December; beaten only 2l over C&D last week, taking Beverley record to 1423234; hasn't scored on Flat since June 2024 but looks a solid contender kept to this venue.
runners.comment: Beaten only 2l over C&D last week, taking Beverley record to 1423234; solid chance

runners.name: Roland Garros
runners.spotlight: Needed a drop to Class 6 to open his account in Catterick event (1m4f, good) last time; back up in grade but may build on that win and also have more to offer in the retained cheekpieces.
runners.comment: Back up in grade but may build on Catterick win and have more to offer in the cheekpieces

runners.name: Naval Tribute
runners.spotlight: Has worn cheekpieces since switched to handicaps, winning at Kempton (1m3f) then below par at Lingfield, both on AW; may rebound and have more to offer, provided he responds well to the fitting of visor.
runners.comment: Scored in first-time cheekpieces on penultimate start; now goes in visor; one to consider

runners.name: Dr Rio
runners.spotlight: Veteran who is 6-23 at Beverley, the most recent success last August in last 1m4f attempt; could go well returned to this scenario with Catterick reappearance (7f) under his belt.
runners.comment: Six course wins, the most recent last August in last 1m4f attempt; could go well

runners.name: Valley Of Flowers
runners.spotlight: Ended last season with a spell of consistent efforts and has frame possibilities provided she returns in similar form; whether she can end a losing spell is another matter (no win since 2023).
runners.comment: Frame possibilities, provided her consistent run of form continues on reappearance

races.race_name: Connexin's 'No Lagging Behind' Handicap (Div I)

runners.name: Tees George
runners.spotlight: Gained sole turf success over C&D (in September 2024) and returns to Beverley in reasonably good form, having made the frame in both outings this year, latest in big field at Doncaster (1m); solid claims.
runners.comment: Gained sole turf success over C&D; made the frame in both starts this year; solid chance

runners.name: Drumcondra
runners.spotlight: Ten-race maiden who has frame possibilities on several pieces of form, including a couple of second-place finishes at Musselburgh last term and fourth-place finish on AW reappearance.
runners.comment: Ten-race maiden who has frame possibilities on several pieces of form

runners.name: No Knee Never
runners.spotlight: Record of 0-8 on turf but he ran well at Yarmouth on penultimate start and readily justified favouritism at Lingfield (1m) on Monday for a third AW win; 5lb penalty is offset by Mason Paetel's useful claim; respected.
runners.comment: Scored readily on AW on Monday; 5lb penalty is offset by Mason Paetel's claim; respected

runners.name: Copper And Five
runners.spotlight: Veteran who gained most recently success in C&D contest (off 5lb higher) last May on second start of his 2025 campaign; reasonably interesting returned to this scenario with Thirsk reappearance under his belt.
runners.comment: Interesting off a handy mark back over this C&D with Thirsk reappearance under his belt
runners.owner: Mrs Sally Doyle And Mrs Ruth Carr

runners.name: Man Is King
runners.spotlight: Holds an each-way squeak, having been placed at Bath (1m) returned to turf last time, but looks no certainty to double his tally; record is just 1-49, the success in AW classified race in February.
runners.comment: Placed at Bath returned to turf last time; recorded sole win in AW classified race

runners.name: Woodleigh
runners.spotlight: Has reverted to inconsistent ways since last September, notwithstanding a Redcar success (1m2f); perhaps needed the run at Doncaster (1m) on reappearance but needs to step up plenty on that effort.
runners.comment: Has reverted to inconsistent ways since last September; others preferred

runners.name: Moonlight Bomb
runners.spotlight: Has lacked consistency for current stable but is only 1lb higher than when recording an account-opening success on AW in December; fighting chance provided he's back in similar form.
runners.comment: Only 1lb higher than when scoring on AW in December; fighting chance if back to form

runners.name: Maple
runners.spotlight: Solid record this year comprises a couple of second-places finishes at Newcastle (1m2f, AW) then success at Sedgefield (2m1f) returned to hurdles; 0-7 on Flat but has possibilities in current form.
runners.comment: Solid record this year comprises two good AW efforts and a hurdles win; possibilities
runners.owner: R Whitelock, L Clemitson & R Jefferson

runners.name: Habari
runners.spotlight: Seven-race maiden whose form has dipped sharply for current yard and who lacks recent practice (absent since last August); best watched unless the betting suggests otherwise.
runners.comment: Seven-race maiden whose form has dipped sharply for current yard

races.race_name: Connexin's 'No Lagging Behind' Handicap (Div II)

runners.name: Dinah Myte
runners.spotlight: Best form, including sole success, on ground softer than good; perhaps unsuited by AW this year but again seems unlikely to have ideal conditions and removal of usual hood presents another question mark.
runners.comment: Best form on ground softer than good; usual hood removed

runners.name: Hashtagnotions
runners.spotlight: Record is 4-23, the most recent win last September in sole C&D attempt (soft); generally consistent since, respectable fifth at Doncaster (1m2f) most recently; gained other turf success on good ground; solid.
runners.comment: Gained most recent win over C&D; generally consistent since; solid chance

runners.name: Coolree
runners.spotlight: Took AW score to 0-16 with two below-par efforts on AW for new stable during the winter; record over C&D, all off higher marks in Class 5, reads 112; interesting back in this scenario and in this lower grade, with a return to form on the cards.
runners.comment: Record over C&D, all off higher marks, reads 112; interesting back in this scenario

runners.name: Glitter Code
runners.spotlight: Sold for 9,000gns since his sole start (2m) for Micky Hammond; still a maiden and drops back markedly in trip but has possibilities on the pick of his form for William Knight; wears new headgear on debut for Ed Bethell; market may offer further guidance.
runners.comment: Still a maiden but has possibilities on the pick of his form; debut for another new yard

runners.name: Sunny Orange
runners.spotlight: Three-time course winner, albeit in the summer months; may not be ready to strike but it's difficult to rule out a good effort with Thirsk reappearance (sixth of 14) under his belt.
runners.comment: Three course wins, albeit in the summer months; sixth of 14 at Thirsk on reappearance

runners.name: Hostelry
runners.spotlight: Couple of duck eggs this term leave a doubt over her current form; however, gained one of her five wins in 2025 over this C&D and she holds a good chance off current mark provided a resurgence materialises; runner-up in this race 12 months ago.
runners.comment: Form over C&D last year comprised a second-place finish in this race and a success

runners.name: Bizarre Law
runners.spotlight: Has an each-way shout, having shown signs of a revival with placed effort at Nottingham (1m) last Saturday returned to turf; whether he can win is another matter, with his strike-rate still poor.
runners.comment: Placed at Nottingham last Saturday returned to turf but still has a poor strike-rate

runners.name: Mini Mac
runners.spotlight: One win apiece for his previous two yards but looks far from solid on 2026 form for another new stable; best watched unless the betting suggests a big run is likely.
runners.comment: Far from solid on 2026 form for new yard; best watched

races.race_name: Racing Again On Bank Holiday Monday Handicap

runners.name: Smoker Bellamy
