runners.spotlight: Five-race maiden who ran well, beating all bar the market leader, at Wolverhampton (9.5f, AW) on seasonal debut; possibilities off same mark, provided he builds on that effort.
runners.comment: Ran well in AW contest on seasonal debut; possibilities

runners.name: Laser Luck
runners.spotlight: Seven-race maiden who has shown no progress since switched to handicaps; however, pedigree suggests this further move upward in trip may spark a bigger effort.
runners.comment: May improve for this further move upward in trip

runners.name: Alice De Clare
runners.spotlight: Thrice-raced filly who is a similar type to Von Dutch, being one whose breeding suggests the switch to turf may prompt progress; interesting handicap debutante.
runners.comment: Switch to turf may prompt progress on handicap debut

runners.name: Jambearee
runners.spotlight: Only eighth of 11 but showed improvement on RPRs at Kempton (1m4f) on handicap/seasonal debut, while shaping as if this return to shorter will suit; could go well.
runners.comment: Showed some improvement on handicap/seasonal debut

runners.name: Hengist Pod
runners.spotlight: Five-race maiden who has inconsistent form and finished last of four on turf debut (1m4f) most recently; however, gives impression this first crack at 1m2f may suit.
runners.comment: Gives impression this first crack at 1m2f may suit

runners.name: Von Dutch
runners.spotlight: Four-race maiden whose AW efforts (8.5f/1m4f) this year suggest this switch to 1m2f will prove optimal; likewise pedigree suggests the switch to turf will suit; interesting.
runners.comment: May improve for the change of trip/surface; interesting

runners.name: Contemplation
runners.spotlight: Modest fourth in 12.5f AW handicap and 1m turf maiden in last two outings (stable debut latest); needs improvement.
runners.comment: Modest fourth on last two starts; needs improvement

runners.name: My Dad Frank
runners.spotlight: Holds weak claims on AW efforts and chance depends on how well he responds to this new scenario, which is a first attempt over 1m2f on turf/handicap debut.
runners.comment: Holds weak claims on AW form; turf/handicap debut
runners.owner: S McMillan, F Reay & A D Brown

runners.name: Barbury Boy
runners.spotlight: Not exactly solid on autumn novice form (7f/1m) but brings sources of possible improvement, namely a gelding operation, switch to handicap level and new trip; not ruled out.
runners.comment: Brings possible sources of improvement; not ruled out

runners.name: Duchess T
runners.spotlight: Far from solid on 2yo maiden form, having finished last in races either side of an effort that may not be reliable; treat with caution unless the market speaks favourably.
runners.comment: Far from solid on 2yo maiden form

runners.name: Lynxman
runners.spotlight: Achieved meagre RPRs last term for Jack Morland, latterly in handicaps; ran below market expectations the final time; needs to improve sharply on debut for new stable.
runners.comment: Needs to improve sharply on stable debut

runners.name: Dream Of Ithaca
runners.spotlight: Handicap debutante who needs to have improved markedly over the winter, having finished last in all 2yo starts.
runners.comment: Finished last in all 2yo starts

races.race_name: Irish Stallion Farms EBF Maiden

runners.name: Real Encounter
runners.spotlight: Ran to a similar level when fourth at Leopardstown (6f) and here (7f) last year, form of latter looking particularly strong now; returned with a neck second to Allsortz 13 days ago (Aughlish 1.25l back in third) here over 6f; drops in trip; should improve for last run and likely to go very close.
runners.comment: Returned with a neck second here over 6f 13 days ago; will improve and likely go close

runners.name: Aughlish
runners.spotlight: Showed ability when fourth on debut here in February and improved markedly on that when beaten 1.5l by Allsortz 13 days ago; showed pace that day so drop in trip should be fine; big player with more improvement to come, albeit tricky draw.
runners.comment: Improved to finish a good third here lto; drop in trip fine but tricky draw; player

runners.name: Namiid
runners.spotlight: Eight-race maiden who ran to a consistent level last year; best effort came when beaten 4l into third behind Ipanema Queen in Listed race at the Curragh in August; bit below form when a beaten fav' at Naas 217 days ago but raced keenly on ground softer than ideal; gelded; surface should suit and sets a good standard.
runners.comment: Eight-race maiden; consistent last year; off 217 days and gelded; sets a good standard

runners.name: Call The Cavalry
runners.spotlight: Ran to a similar level first two starts when fifth and second over C&D; improved stepped up to 7f when beaten 3l into fifth by Glitter And Glory 34 days ago; trip likely too sharp but each-way claims.
runners.comment: Improved for step up to 7f when fifth here lto; this trip likely too sharp

runners.name: Falcon Queen
runners.spotlight: Too keen over 7f here on debut in December when a well-held fourth; improved a bit dropped in trip in a hood after a break 22 days ago when beaten 4.25l by Ocean's Breath; drops in trip; should step forward again but needs to.
runners.comment: Settled better in hood when fourth 22 days ago; drop in trip fine but needs improvement

runners.name: Frosted Sky
runners.spotlight: No Nay Never filly; dam Listed-placed 6f winner (RPR 98), half-sister to winners Violet's Lads (7f-1m2f) and The Big Bad (6f 2yo), out of German/Belgian 7f-1m1f winner; trainer can ready them; market check.
runners.comment: No Nay Never filly; dam Listed-placed 6f winner (RPR 98); trainer can ready them

runners.name: Atallah
runners.spotlight: Hasn't shown much to get excited in two starts, not beating a rival either occasion, including at this track 13 days ago; down in trip; unlikely to figure.
runners.comment: Hasn't shown much to get excited in two starts; unlikely to figure

runners.name: Beach Barbie
runners.spotlight: Footstepsinthesand filly out of Magic Bear, a 5f/6f winner (including at this track) for the stable; didn't do anything to suggest she's ready to win a maiden when sixth of seven here 14 days ago (beaten 8l), albeit very green; down in trip; should come on for that but likely not enough to threaten.
runners.comment: Very green on debut here, well-held sixth of seven; should improve but likely not enough
runners.owner: N W T Dalzell & Edward Lynam

runners.name: She's A Gift
runners.spotlight: 13-race maiden, six of those coming at this track; best effort when fourth over C&D in handicap off a basement mark in January; little chance of winning a maiden.
runners.comment: Very modest 13-race maiden; best run came over C&D but little chance of winning a maiden

races.race_name: Irishinjuredjockeys.com Handicap

runners.name: Baby Basma
runners.spotlight: Won a 5f Cork maiden on debut in April 2025, form of which nothing special; returned from nearly a year off the track 41 days ago, ran ok to be beaten 9.5l into fourth by Flanker Jet; will improve for that and opening mark looks quite fair.
runners.comment: Cork maiden winner over 5f; returned from long absence with ok run lto; mark looks fair

runners.name: Love Bomb
runners.spotlight: Showed ability last year in maidens and scored on handicap debut over 5f at Down Royal; ran ok on seasonal debut at Naas before finishing 11th of 13 here over 6f 22 days ago, 6l behind winner Stone Bear; drop back to 5f will suit and could run well.
runners.comment: Did too much in front lto over 6f, but only beaten 6l; could run well back in trip

runners.name: Plushy
runners.spotlight: Bounced back to form of her maiden runs when 1.75l second to Stone Bear here over 6f in January; followed that up 15 days later with a third over the same C&D; 13lb better off with Stone Bear than when they last met; likes to be ridden forcefully and dangerous back in trip for trainer in flying form.
runners.comment: Enjoying positive tactics; 13lb better off with Stone Bear than for 1.75l second in Jan
runners.owner: Derek Murray/Poetry In Motion Syndicate/Patrick McGuinness

runners.name: Lady Landhort
runners.spotlight: Improved to spring 33-1 surprise when beating Jazzy Dancer by 0.5l over 6f here last month; ran fine when beaten 4.5l into eighth off 4lb higher 22 days ago; drops in trip and has a squeak under good claimer.
runners.comment: Scored here over 6f last month; ran ok off 4lb higher lto; drops in trip and a squeak
runners.owner: T J Flanagan & Ronan Macken & Shane McSweeney & S O'Malley

runners.name: Subiaco
runners.spotlight: C&D winner on handicap debut in January, beating subsequent winner An Laochmor by 1.5l; backed that up off 7lb higher when beaten under 2l into fourth next time over same C&D; below form on turf last time out but ground an obvious excuse; chance.
runners.comment: C&D winner who backed that up off 7lb higher when fourth in Feb; ground too soft lto

runners.name: Luminessa
runners.spotlight: Fair form in trio of maidens, latest when beaten 3.25l into fifth by Oxford Circus 55 days ago; travelled nicely that day so drop back to 5f looks a good move; opening mark doesn't look harsh; definite chance in first-time tongue-tie.
runners.comment: Travelled nicely before flattening out over 6f 55 days ago; nice mark on h'cap debut
runners.owner: Scott Sterling & Darragh Cuffe & Conor Keohane

runners.name: Boston Max
runners.spotlight: Well-held in three maidens last year, sent off at three-figure prices the last twice; not much to recommend him on so far but starts in handicaps off low mark for top trainer; drops in trip; first-time tongue-tie; off 201 days and gelded.
runners.comment: Low-key three starts in maidens; top trainer; h'cap debut; first-time tongue-strap; gelded

runners.name: Hooves Your Daddy
runners.spotlight: Best effort in six starts came by some way when fifth in Naas maiden in August (6f, good); beat one rival home in two handicap starts since; off 206 days; dropped 4lb but needs revival.
runners.comment: Best effort came in Naas maiden but way out of form last twice in h'caps; off 206 days

races.race_name: New Saddle Bar At Dundalk Stadium Handicap

runners.name: Iryna's Star
runners.spotlight: Best effort in ten starts came here over 7f on second handicap start when beaten 2.75l into third by Beau Army (Queen Leila in second); poor in five starts since but mark has dropped accordingly and step back up in trip should help.
runners.comment: Best effort when third here over 7f in Jan; poor since but mark has dropped; up in trip

runners.name: Cause I Like You
runners.spotlight: Couple of fair runs in maidens at this track over 5f/6f; poor run on soft ground at Naas last month but fared better back on the AW 22 days ago over 6f, staying on into fifth, beaten 3l by Stone Bear; on a good mark and this trip could unlock improvement.
runners.comment: Back to form on the AW lto, staying on well over 6f at this track; can offer more over 1m

runners.name: Beau Army
runners.spotlight: Dual course winner over 6f/7f last year (beat Queen Leila and Iryna's Star in January); curbed by increase in rating last twice on AW, finishing sixth both occasions; underwhelming run on turf 15 days ago but soft ground an obvious excuse; steps up in trip and can be thereabouts.
runners.comment: Dual course winner; seemed to have found limit; step up in trip needs to bring improvement

runners.name: Queen Leila
runners.spotlight: Encouraging second over 7f here in January; below form in two subseqeunt starts; well-backed and fared better ridden more positively 13 days ago when beaten 2.75l into fourth by Royal County Glory; on an okay mark but step up to 1m not guaranteed to suit.
runners.comment: Good second here over 7f in Jan; below form after but okay run when fourth lto; up in trip

runners.name: Keskon
runners.spotlight: Nothing of note in maidens, or on handicap debut over C&D in December; ran better after a break when beaten 3l into seventh over 6f here 41 days ago; back up in trip; hard to fancy.
runners.comment: Weak maiden form, ran okay over 6f here 41 days ago; up in trip; makes no real appeal

runners.name: Wedding Year
runners.spotlight: Showed next to nothing in three turf maidens and a nursery but improved switched to AW over 7f last twice; beaten 4l into fourth by Beau Army 83 days ago; should stay a mile.
runners.comment: Improved for siwtch to AW last twice; beaten 4l by Beau Army lto; a mile should suit

runners.name: Brave Approach
runners.spotlight: Showed little in three maidens before making a winning handicap debut over C&D last month, staying on well to beat Kc Bear by 0.75l; well placed that day and form of that questionable; failed to back it up when well beaten 34 days ago; not one of the obvious ones.
runners.comment: Winning h'cap debut over C&D last month; form questionable and failed to back up lto

runners.name: Caoilte
runners.spotlight: Made decent debut over C&D in January, below that form twice in maidens after; beaten 4.75l on handicap debut here over 7f 13 days ago, finished off okay having been outpaced; step up in trip will suit; each-way claims.
runners.comment: Ran okay on h'cap debut, sticking on over 7f; step up in trip will suit; ew claims

runners.name: Elora Prince
runners.spotlight: Nursery form on turf left a lot to be desired last year but ran much better upped to this trip when beaten 4.5l into fourth by Keepsgettingbetter last month over C&D; poor run again on turf 12 days ago in first-time blinkers; can bounce back on the AW and an each-way contender.
runners.comment: Poor form on turf overall but good fourth over C&D last month gives him a squeak

runners.name: Push Harder
runners.spotlight: Caught the eye when staying on into fifth over C&D on handicap debut in first-time hood in November; backed that up next time when beaten 2.75l into third by John La Bear; dropped 2lb; off 141 days; one of the more solid options.
runners.comment: Improved in h'caps, running well over C&D last twice; down 2lb; off 141 days; solid

races.race_name: View Restaurant At Dundalk Stadium Handicap

runners.name: Desert Of The Sea
runners.spotlight: Second runner for yard; cheap purchase out of Ed Bethell's yard; has found his level since coming down from a peak Irish rating of 56; runner-up over 7f here in January before hitting the frame twice over C&D; faded late after forceful ride from wide draw on latest; player from inside draw.
runners.comment: Consistent form in defeat here this year and had excuses for latest seventh; player

runners.name: Roman Harry
runners.spotlight: Gained just second win from 39 starts when springing 50-1 shock in 7f course claimer in December; creditable efforts in two handicaps here since for new yard; below best on softish ground at Leopardstown eight days ago but player back on this surface.
runners.comment: Below best on softish ground at Leopardstown 8 days ago but player back on this surface

runners.name: Famous Enough
runners.spotlight: C&D winner in September from 7lb lower; got going too late when beaten favourite at Cork end of last term; slowly away and eased once chance was gone on return at Gowran a fortnight ago; should be sharper now.
runners.comment: C&D winner; slowly away and eased once chance was gone on return at Gowran

runners.name: Master Garvey
runners.spotlight: Has done a lot of racing since C&D win off 47 just over a year ago, one of only two wins in 48-race career; solid third in C&D handicap earlier this month; raced keenly from wide draw at Gowran on latest; contender at his best.
runners.comment: Third in C&D handicap earlier this month; raced keenly from wide draw at Gowran on latest
runners.owner: Patrick M O'Brien/Larry Murray/Richard W Walsh/Derek Cummins

runners.name: Imperial Fighter
runners.spotlight: Two minor wins on Flat in Britain; failed to build on C&D second in January; best of six subsequent runs when fourth in C&D claimer; well beaten here (7f) on latest; new headgear is tried now.
runners.comment: Failed to build on C&D 2nd in January; best of 6 subsequent runs when 4th in C&D claimer

runners.name: Rampage
runners.spotlight: Four-time winner, including on turf early in his career; won over C&D from current mark during a busy 2025 campaign; three runs on turf this year, a few lengths off Comfort Line when eighth of 20 at Leopardstown 11 days ago.
runners.comment: C&D winner; few lengths off Comfort Line when eighth of 20 at Leopardstown 11 days ago
runners.owner: Mrs P McCourt/Whyte's Stamullen Syndicate/S Sweeney

runners.name: Cosmic Force
runners.spotlight: Maiden after 15 starts; placed only twice, including on latest when never-nearer third of 12 here (7f) 13 days ago; each-way player if he sees out the extra furlong from unchanged mark.
runners.comment: Good third on latest; each-way player if he sees out the extra furlong from unchanged mark

runners.name: Bang Po
runners.spotlight: Heavy-ground winner in September 2023 but no joy since; has struggled in two Dundalk runs this year; 5lb wrong.
runners.comment: Heavy-ground winner in 2023 but no joy since; struggled in 2 course runs this year

runners.name: Comfort Line
runners.spotlight: Six-time course winner with his last win coming over 7f here in March last year off 72; modest 3-71 strike-rate on turf but excellent third of 20 over 1m at Leopardstown 11 days ago, headed in closing stages; big player in current mood under 10lb claimer.
runners.comment: Six-time course winner ran an excellent race on turf latest; big player in current mood

runners.name: Mullacash Buzz
runners.spotlight: Six-time winner (four on AW) but last win came here in 2023; never a factor in either course outing this year.
runners.comment: Six-time winner but last win came here in 2023; never a factor in 2 course runs this year

runners.name: Sonoma Sunset
runners.spotlight: Weak maiden form; big price when failing to make any impression at the Curragh on handicap debut; tailed off on AW debut in November but was drawn wide on first try at 10.5f; returns from break with Colin Keane booked so market must be monitored; cheekpieces.
runners.comment: Weak form in maidens and handicaps but Colin Keane booked for return and cheekpieces on

races.race_name: Put The Fun In Fundraising At Dundalk Maiden

runners.name: I Hope You Dance
runners.spotlight: Showed ability when eighth on racecourse debut at Cork in September; matched that form when beaten 15.5l behind potentially smart rival 187 days ago; form of that maiden looks strong; can figure if handling step up in trip.
runners.comment: Showed ability in two 1m maidens last year; can figure if handling step up in trip

runners.name: Mendez
runners.spotlight: Promising debut when 3.5l fourth here over 1m 48 days ago behind Son Of Beauty, despite being unconsidered in market; not given a hard time and will improve; steps up in trip and should be thereabouts.
runners.comment: Promising debut, fourth here over 1m; will improve; up in trip; should be thereabouts

runners.name: Bell Ex Too
runners.spotlight: Nice pedigree and fared okay in what looked a strong maiden on debut on soft at the Curragh 26 days ago, beaten 17.5l into sixth; improvement likely up in trip and not discounted.
runners.comment: Nice pedigree; ran okay on debut in strong maiden at the Curragh; not discounted

runners.name: Changing Lanes
runners.spotlight: 125,000euros yearling; Galiway colt; half-brother to winners Rising Up (French 6.5f-1m inc AW; RPR 91), Popeye Doyle (1m/8.6f AW; 76) and Catalyzed (US 8.5f turf); dam French 6f-1m winner (inc 2yo/Listed; 100), half-sister to French 6f-9.5f winner Red Crazy; in good hands and trainer in form.
runners.comment: 125,000euros yearling, half-brother three winners (6.5f-8.5f); makes debut; stable in form

runners.name: Druids Glen
runners.spotlight: Frankel colt, brother to yard's top-class Irish 1,000 Guineas winner Lake Victoria; very green on debut at Leopardstown in October when fifth; small step forward when 12l sixth to James J Braddock on heavy 172 days ago, still green; steps up in trip in first-time cheekpieces; another step forward likely and obvious claims.
runners.comment: Brother to Lake Victoria; work in progress first two starts; cheekpieces; big chance
runners.owner: Derrick Smith & Mrs John Magnier & Michael Tabor

runners.name: Sir Trancealot
runners.spotlight: Raced freely before fading on heavy ground at the Curragh 26 days ago, finishing last of ten; should be capable of better but big step forward required.
runners.comment: Last of ten on debut on heavy 26 days ago; needs big step forward

runners.name: Bay Of Stars
runners.spotlight: Well-bred Irish Derby entry ran to just a fair level when beaten 10l over 1m here 153 days ago; looked in need of experience and should improve plenty, but needs to upped in distance.
runners.comment: Irish Derby entry; ran to just a fair level on debut 153 days ago, green; will improve

races.race_name: DundalkStadium.com Handicap

runners.name: Drucker
runners.spotlight: Has enjoyed a good winter campaign here, consistent other than when sixth behind Voice Of Reason in November; won in good style over C&D in February; big efforts over 2m and C&D since; respected again.
runners.comment: Seldom runs a bad race and impressive C&D win in February; should be bang there again

runners.name: Rock Of Ireland
runners.spotlight: Off the mark over this trip at the Curragh on yard debut last month under a positive ride in testing conditions by a diminishing margin; good effort at Gowran from 7lb higher a fortnight ago when runner-up (well-backed); not certain to replicate that form on AW.
runners.comment: In great form on turf in testing conditions; not certain to replicate that form on AW

runners.name: Mont St Michel
runners.spotlight: By Galileo; 2m1f Killarney win last summer; out of depth in Group 2 Prix Foy and well below best on final start for Aidan O'Brien; fair run when thrown in at the deep end for hurdles/stable debut at Leopardstown; struggled at Thurles; AW/handicap debut; new headgear.
runners.comment: Ex-Ballydoyle; won at Killarney over 2m1f last summer; some promise hurdling for this yard
runners.owner: Patrick John O'Brien & J Carthy & M G Hogan

runners.name: Wandering Rocks
runners.spotlight: Sole success for this yard came in Ballinrobe maiden hurdle in 2024; fine effort at big odds over C&D after long layoff in January when fourth to Voice Of Reason but unable to back that up in two subsequent starts; new headgear.
runners.comment: Good C&D run after layoff in January when 4th to Voice Of Reason; unable to back that up

runners.name: Beauparc
runners.spotlight: Dual C&D winner, gaining fourth career win here last month off 75, plenty to spare when beating Voice Of Reason; good effort when third from this mark in follow-up bid; Colin Keane takes over now; shortlisted.
runners.comment: Easy C&D win last month and solid third from revised mark since; Colin Keane on now

runners.name: Sir Callisto
runners.spotlight: Dual C&D winner, including when improving on last month's comeback third behind Beauparc to run out a cosy winner off 72 at the start of the month; 5lb higher now but there's potential for more to come.
runners.comment: Won off 72 at the start of the month; 5lb higher now but potential for more to come

runners.name: Liberate
runners.spotlight: Second runner for yard; won 1m2f Goodwood novice last year but safely held afterwards in handicaps for James Horton; bought for 5,000gns in December; never a factor behind Sir Callisto on C&D stable debut; will need more time.
runners.comment: Bought for 5,000gns in December; never a factor behind Sir Callisto on C&D stable debut

runners.name: San Andreas
runners.spotlight: Course regular (8-35) who is now at the veteran stage; no faulting his two runner-up efforts over 1m4f/2m here this year; always behind in valuable 1m2f Lingfield contest at Easter; solid claims back at his favoured venue.
runners.comment: Always behind in valuable Lingfield race at Easter; solid claims back at favoured venue

runners.name: Hollys Graces
runners.spotlight: Lightly raced 4yo won 7f course maiden on debut in September 2024; well held in four runs last year in good-class company; return to AW could spark a revival on handicap debut but it will need to.
runners.comment: Course debut win; return to AW could spark a revival on handicap debut but it will need to

runners.name: La Dame Blanche
runners.spotlight: Three-time Flat winner during her time with two previous trainers; weak overall form in handicaps for this stable; satisfactory hurdles runs late last year; hard to fancy on second AW start (tailed off only other try).
runners.comment: Three-time Flat winner but hard to fancy on second AW start (tailed off only other try)

races.race_name: Racing Again May 26th Handicap

runners.name: Fine Print
runners.spotlight: Second runner for yard; won twice at Wolverhampton a year ago (1m6f/2m); didn't pull up any trees over hurdles last year; looked in need of the run when down the field here (1m4f) this month; market can separate yard's runners.
runners.comment: Second runner for yard; won twice at Wolverhampton a year ago but hard to gauge

runners.name: Barretstown
runners.spotlight: Four-time course winner (three over C&D) with his last win coming in September off 1lb higher; best of four 2026 runs came over C&D last month when beaten 0.75l into third; below best two weeks later; player at his best.
runners.comment: 3-time C&D winner; best of 2026 runs came over C&D last month when beaten 0.75l into third

runners.name: Tara Power
runners.spotlight: Turf record is poor (0-26) but more effective on AW (2-25); this year's form hasn't offered much but is 8lb below last winning mark so can't be totally discounted.
runners.comment: This year's form hasn't offered much but 8lb below last winning mark; can't be discounted

runners.name: Ferrari Desert
runners.spotlight: Sole win came here over 7f in October 2024 and twice second here over 1m in opening months of 2025; no worthwhile form yet this year and has never raced beyond the mile; others preferred.
runners.comment: No worthwhile form yet this year and has never raced beyond the mile; others preferred

runners.name: Doctor Grace
runners.spotlight: Second runner for yard; has won here and regularly placed; however, recent efforts have been below-par and was behind Jawhary last time; others have stronger credentials.
runners.comment: Has won here; however, recent efforts have been below-par and was behind Jawhary latest

runners.name: Electric Beauty
runners.spotlight: Won her third race on turf when taking 1m Curragh handicap (heavy) in March before a close second there next time; disappointing favourite at Gowran on latest; 0-7 on AW but has run well on the surface and has to be taken seriously from 19lb lower AW mark.
runners.comment: Took form to new heights on turf; has to be taken seriously from 19lb lower AW mark

runners.name: If Dora Could
runners.spotlight: Not too much to take from three maiden runs on turf last year before staying on into midfield in C&D handicap in August; ran no race at Navan in October; tongue-tied ahead of return; others hold more solid claims.
runners.comment: Midfield in C&D handicap in August; ran no race at Navan in October; tongue-tie for return

runners.name: Eastern Wind
runners.spotlight: Five-time Flat winner (0-8 on AW) and 1-6 over hurdles; decent return from a break when fifth over C&D last month but finished last over hurdles at Bellewstown five days ago.
runners.comment: Fifth over C&D last month but finished last over hurdles at Bellewstown five days ago

runners.name: Jawhary
runners.spotlight: Dual course winner (1m4f) during the winter; beaten only 1.5l over C&D off 54 last month before runner-up here over 1m4f on latest; looks sure to give his running once again; shortlisted.
runners.comment: Dual course winner (1m4f) has been very consistent in defeat since; sure to go well again

runners.name: Copper Craft
runners.spotlight: Improved for fitting of headgear here over 1m last year (left off now), third of 14 in September after making good headway from off the pace and again rattled home well to dead-heat for fourth in December; should relish the step up in trip on his return.
runners.comment: Big eyecatcher over shorter last year and should relish this sort of trip on that evidence

runners.name: Lady Luck
runners.spotlight: Moderate efforts in handicaps to date although had an excuse here over 1m4f when last seen as was 12lb wrong; nonetheless, remains hard to fancy.
runners.comment: Had an excuse here over 1m4f when last seen as was 12lb wrong; remains hard to fancy

runners.name: Yup Bro
runners.spotlight: Lightly-raced 4yo made mildly encouraging handicap debut over C&D in November but beaten out of sight when well-backed favourite here when upped to 1m4f the following month; clearly much better was expected so cheekpieces may help and stable among the winners.
runners.comment: Mildly encouraging C&D run before awful run over 1m4f when favourite; cheekpieces now

races.race_name: Southwell Cardinal Conditions Stakes (GBB Race)

runners.name: Yazin
runners.spotlight: Built on his debut effort when ending up a comfortable winner at Redcar (7f, good to soft) in September; took another step forward when winning a novice under a penalty at Newmarket (7f, good) in October when last seen, form that has been franked with six of those behind winning since; had wind surgery since; stepping up in trip should prove no issue and he looks the one to beat on his return.
runners.comment: Made it 2-3 when winning at Newmarket (form franked); the one to beat on his return

runners.name: Golden View
runners.spotlight: Dam 7f-1m winner (including 2yo/AW/German Group 3; RPR 107); made a winning debut when sent off odds-on favourite at Kempton (7f, AW) in December, pulling well clear with the runner-up who has won since; should have no problem with this step up in trip and he looks a strong challenger to Yazin.
runners.comment: Pulled well clear with a subsequent winner on his debut at Kempton (7f); has strong claims

runners.name: Intense Vision
runners.spotlight: Dam 7f 2yo winner (RPR 90); 2-2 in France, winning a maiden at Craon (6.5f, soft) in September then a conditions event at Bordeaux Le Bouscat (1m, very soft) in October; has been off since and now makes his stable/British debut having been purchased for 60,000gns; watch the betting.
runners.comment: 2-2 in France, winning over 6.5f and 1m on soft ground; makes his stable/British debut

races.race_name: Celebrate With Sponsorship At Southwell Racecourse Handicap

runners.name: Life After Love
runners.spotlight: Has form figures of 121 over 5f, with both of the wins coming over this C&D, the most recent by a neck 28 days ago; the third has won since and she's respected off this 4lb higher mark.
runners.comment: Form figures of 121 over 5f (both wins over C&D); respected off this 4lb higher mark

runners.name: Blue Deveron
runners.spotlight: Beaten a neck over this C&D in January on debut but he failed to build on that at Wolverhampton next time; left the impression this drop back to 5f would suit when third at Chelmsford (6f, AW; second has won since) 28 days ago; contender on this handicap debut.
runners.comment: Beaten a neck over C&D on debut and this drop back to 5f should suit on h'cap debut

runners.name: Sands Of Seve
runners.spotlight: Got off the mark when making all in a first-time visor at Wolverhampton (5f, AW) last month but he was comfortably held at Lingfield 27 days ago having been raised 9lb for that success; needs to bounce back.
runners.comment: Got off the mark in a first-time visor at Wolverhampton last month; disappointing latest

runners.name: Beach Partee
runners.spotlight: Won three times for William Knight, two of which came on the AW; runner-up at Lingfield (5f) in January but he failed to built a rival at Bath (5f, good) on his stable debut 18 days ago; needs to take a step forward from that effort.
runners.comment: Runner-up at Lingfield in January but failed to beat a rival on his stable debut

runners.name: Believeinmenow
runners.spotlight: Failed to progress from his promising debut last year but he was beaten only 3l when ninth of 19 in a competitive nursery at York (6f, good to firm; first-time hood) last August; had been gelded prior to a a respectable reappearance at Bath (5f, good; raced freely) 18 days ago; now makes his AW debut with solid claims.
runners.comment: Failed to progress from his debut last year but ran respectably at Bath on his return

races.race_name: Dog Friendly Raceday Tues 2nd June Handicap

runners.name: Swinging London
runners.spotlight: Dual C&D winner; beaten 2l when fourth of six here last month and he was unfortunate not to go even closer when fourth of 12 at Pontefract (1m4f, good to soft; short of room late on) 16 days ago; switches back to the AW with a good chance.
runners.comment: Dual C&D winner who was unfortunate not to go closer at Pontefract last time; good claims

runners.name: Captain Brett
runners.spotlight: Showed improvement when stepping up in trip last year, including a win at Yarmouth (1m6f, good); disappointing at Newmarket (1m6f, good to firm) when last seen 257 days ago but the break may have done him some good.
runners.comment: Won over 1m6f at Yarmouth last year; disappointing when last seen but may bounce back

runners.name: The Truant
runners.spotlight: Five-time winner in Ireland (hurdles/Flat) but he was well held in two starts for this yard at the end of last year; however, he seemed to be going quite well at the top of the straight before weakening into seventh at Kempton (2m, AW) 17 days ago and may take a step forward from that return to action.
runners.comment: Five-time winner in Ireland; gives impression he may come on from his return at Kempton

runners.name: John Galt
runners.spotlight: Shaped with encouragement on his handicap debut at Wolverhampton (1m4f, AW) last month but didn't help himself by hanging left when third of nine in a novice here over 1m4f 18 days ago (tongue-tie and cheekpieces were on for the first time); looks worth a try over this longer trip and he can be competitive returned to a handicap.
runners.comment: Hung left when third in a novice here over 1m4f last time; looks worth a go over this trip

runners.name: Vaguely Royal
runners.spotlight: Didn't run too badly when sixth here over 2m2f on his penultimate start but he was well held at Lingfield (2m, AW) 20 days ago; without a win on the Flat since the end of 2023 and needs to bounce back.
runners.comment: Well held at Lingfield last time and is without a win on the Flat since the end of 2023

races.race_name: Book The Bistro In Sherwood Restaurant Handicap (GBBPlus Race)

runners.name: New York Minute
runners.spotlight: Won his last two starts for Ralph Beckett and completed the hat-trick here over 2m in November when visored for the first time on his debut for this stable; ran another good race when runner-up at Kempton (2m) next time but he was below his best 97 days ago; could bounce back on his first start since but this drop back in trip may not be ideal.
runners.comment: Three-time winner on the AW (1m6f-2m); below his best when last seen; drops back in trip

runners.name: Marnier
runners.spotlight: Off the mark at the sixth attempt when beating two rivals in good style in a maiden at Lingfield (1m4f, AW) in November but he dropped away up the straight having taken a grip in a handicap at Kempton on his return 17 days ago; needs to build on that.
runners.comment: Beat two rivals in a maiden at Lingfield in November; needs to build on his Kempton return

runners.name: Desert Shadow
runners.spotlight: First past the post (demoted to second having caused interference) in a handicap at Beverley last August but he failed to beat a rival at Meydan (1m4f, good) 41 days ago; dropping back in trip should suit and he may bounce back on his return to Britain.
runners.comment: First past the post at Beverley last year; failed to beat a rival at Meydan; back in trip

runners.name: Yaa Min
runners.spotlight: Got off to the perfect start for this stable when narrowly coming out on top at Lingfield (1m4f, AW) last month but wasn't able to build on that at Yarmouth (1m2f, good) 12 days ago; place claims back on the AW.
runners.comment: Made a winning stable debut at Lingfield last month; couldn't build on that at Yarmouth

runners.name: Lady Ridgewood
runners.spotlight: Finished third of five at Brighton (1m2f, good to firm) on her handicap debut last April, form that has been well franked by the winner, and got off the mark at the fifth attempt when landing a maiden at Chelmsford (1m2f, AW) on her final start for Peter Chapple-Hyam; has since changed yards (same owner) having been off since and could have more to offer.
runners.comment: Has switched yards since winning at Chelmsford in August; should have more to offer

runners.name: Elsass
runners.spotlight: Sole win came by a nose at Chelmsford (1m2f, AW) last August and he ran well when second of nine at Kempton (1m3f, AW) next time; possibly didn't handle the undulations of Newmarket (1m4f) in the Old Rowley Cup when last seen and has since been gelded; could be capable of bouncing back returned to the AW.
runners.comment: Possibly didn't handle Newmarket when last seen; gelded since; could bounce back

races.race_name: Come Racing For Free 18th June Handicap

runners.name: Raspoutine
runners.spotlight: Held in four starts on turf last year but he posted a much improved effort when switched to the AW on his handicap debut at Lingfield nine days ago, kicking off the bend before being eased late on; 6lb well-in under a penalty and he's the one to beat.
runners.comment: Posted a much improved effort when winning comfortably on his h'cap debut; the one to beat

runners.name: Aneirin's Sword
runners.spotlight: Below his best at Nottingham (8.5f, good) 15 days ago but he had been in good form on the AW prior to that, with form figures of 1222123 since the beginning of the year; may bounce back returned to the AW with blinkers on for the first time.
runners.comment: Below his best on turf latest but was in good form on the AW prior to that; blinkers on

runners.name: Lexington Express
runners.spotlight: Posted an improved effort when winning by 6l at Wolverhampton (8.5f, AW) last month, despite hanging right, and backed that up when going close over that same C&D 19 days ago; can again go well.
runners.comment: Won by 6l at Wolverhampton last month and went close there again last time; can go well

runners.name: Clear Above
runners.spotlight: Ran to a similar level in two maiden events at Newcastle and Southwell (both over 7f), but he failed to build on those efforts on his handicap debut at Wolverhampton (7f, AW) two weeks ago; steps up to 1m with better needed.
runners.comment: Failed to build on his maiden efforts when fourth of five on his handicap debut; up to 1m

runners.name: Action Reaction
runners.spotlight: Struggled in three maiden events (1m/7f) in March and April (beaten 33l and 22l in two of them); much better is needed on her handicap debut but the betting should be informative.
runners.comment: Struggled in three maiden events; much better needed on handicap debut; watch the betting

runners.name: Cobalt Comet
runners.spotlight: Sent off big prices and never threatened in three novice events at this track (two over C&D); open to improvement but he's best watched on his handicap debut.
runners.comment: Sent off big prices and never threatened in three novice events here; handicap debut

races.race_name: Conference & Events At Southwell Racecourse Restricted Novice Stakes (Bands B, C and D) (GBB Race)

runners.name: Red Spells Danger
runners.spotlight: Showed ability in all four starts last year, including when sixth in a competitive race at York in October, and ran another fine race when second of 11 in a maiden at Catterick (7f, good) on his return 15 days ago; good claims if coping with the switch to AW.
runners.comment: Showed ability in all four starts last year and ran another fine race on his return

runners.name: Ay Up Duck
runners.spotlight: Encouraging debut when chasing home the red-hot favourite in a C&D maiden on his debut and he ran to a similar level here over 5f two weeks ago; has hung right in both starts but should benefit from stepping back up to this trip.
runners.comment: Runner-up over 7f/5f here on his two starts; steps back up in distance

runners.name: Got The Booty
runners.spotlight: Built on her debut when second of ten at Kempton (6f, AW) in September and that form looks solid (split two subsequent winners); pulled clear with the winner when beaten a neck here over 6f on her return two weeks ago and now steps up in trip which may bring about a bit more improvement.
runners.comment: Pulled clear with the winner when beaten a neck here over 6f on her return; steps up to 7f

runners.name: Irish Incentive
runners.spotlight: Went close in a novice over C&D last month before finishing fifth at Kempton over 1m 17 days ago, not picking up as well as might have been expected early in the straight; the drop back in trip should suit and she has cheekpieces added.
runners.comment: Fifth at Kempton last time but she went close over this C&D last month; cheekpieces on

runners.name: Rogue Desire
runners.spotlight: Shaped with promise on her debut at Salisbury (7f, good to firm) last August but she wasn't able to build on that on her comeback run at Wolverhampton (7f, AW) 19 days ago; may come on for that but she'll need to.
runners.comment: Showed promise on her debut last year but failed to match that on her comeback run

runners.name: Turton
runners.spotlight: 3yo by Ribchester; sixth foal; half-brother to 6f/7f AW winner Whittle Le Woods (RPR 96); dam 7f-10.4f winner (92), half-sister to useful winners Credit Swap (6f-1m1f), Lockantanks (7f-1m) and Seafront (6f), out of 5f 2yo winner; his half-brother won seven times on the AW and he's an interesting newcomer; watch the betting.
runners.comment: Half-brother to seven-time AW winner Whittle Le Woods; watch the betting on his debut

runners.name: Katie's Song
runners.spotlight: Second foal; dam 1m4f winner (RPR 54), half-sister to winners Continga (6f 2yo), Flanagans (Italian 5f) and Lily Trotter (2m hurdle), out of Group-placed 7f 2yo winner; one to note in the betting on her debut.
runners.comment: Second foal; dam 1m4f winner (RPR 54); one to note in the betting on her debut

runners.name: Marquessofanglesey
runners.spotlight: Dam 7f/1m winner (RPR 83); showed some ability when sixth of 13 at Kempton (1m, AW; 9-1) in October; drops in trip on his first start since and he's open to improvement.
runners.comment: Showed some ability when sixth at Kempton over 1m on his debut; open to improvement
runners.owner: Julie Martin & David R Martin & Partner

runners.name: Beau Chevalier
runners.spotlight: 150-1 and he was always behind following a slow start at Ascot (7f, good to firm) on his debut last summer; switches to the AW having been off since.
runners.comment: Always behind on his debut at Ascot last summer; switches to the AW having been off since

races.race_name: Ladies Day Sunday 16th August Handicap

runners.name: Mwafaq
runners.spotlight: Won two 7f handicaps at Wolverhampton in October and completed the hat-trick when winning on his return at Thirsk (7f, good) 13 days ago; can remain competitive back on the AW following a 2lb rise.
runners.comment: Completed a hat-trick when winning at Thirsk on his return; up 2lb; can remain competitive
runners.owner: Richard Fahey Ebor Racing Club Ltd

runners.name: Enpassant
runners.spotlight: Gained his third success on the AW when beating On Key over C&D eight days ago, despite hanging left; holds solid claims under a 5lb penalty (due to be 1lb higher in future).
runners.comment: Gained his third AW win when beating On Key over C&D last time; solid claims under penalty

runners.name: Searchingtheblues
runners.spotlight: Won her first two starts in 7f novice events on the AW but she's mostly struggled since, including when beating only one home at Lingfield (7f, AW) 20 days ago; drops in grade with her mark continuing to fall.
runners.comment: Mostly struggled since entering handicaps; drops in grade with her mark continuing to fall

runners.name: Bomb Squad
runners.spotlight: Eight wins on the AW include three at this track (6f/7f), the last win coming off 2lb lower at Wolverhampton last month; ran well when runner-up here 16 days ago and there's no reason why he can't go well again.
runners.comment: Three-time course winner; ran well when runner-up here 16 days ago; can again go well

runners.name: Louie The Legend
runners.spotlight: Two wins from 21 starts; went close at Nottingham (8.5f, heavy) in October but he's struggled since (Flat/jumps), including when well beaten at that same track 15 days ago; drops back to 7f; others are preferred.
runners.comment: Beaten a neck at Nottingham in October but he's struggled since; others are preferred

runners.name: Berning Hot
runners.spotlight: Sole win from 15 starts came at Brighton (7f, good) last August but she ran well when third of nine at Kempton (1m, AW) in January; however, she was below her best there 78 days ago and now drops back in trip.
runners.comment: 1-15; ran well when third at Kempton in January but below her best there last time

runners.name: Fools Rush In
runners.spotlight: Gained his second C&D success when winning off a 2lb higher mark in February but he's been down the field in four starts since, including when missing the break at Lingfield 27 days ago; return to form is needed.
runners.comment: Down the field in four starts since winning over C&D in February; return to form needed
runners.owner: S Thompson And Mrs R Carr

runners.name: On Key
runners.spotlight: 16-race maiden but he ran well, albeit did have every chance, when runner-up behind Enpassant over C&D eight days ago; place claims.
runners.comment: 0-16 but he ran well when runner-up behind Enpassant over C&D eight days ago; place claims

runners.name: Mereside Madness
runners.spotlight: Won four times for his previous stable, three of which came on the AW at Newcastle; ended his time with that yard with some lesser efforts and he's been well held in two starts for Scott Dixon; dropped another 3lb.
runners.comment: Won four times for his previous stable but well held in two starts for this yard

races.race_name: Summer Social Events At Southwell Racecourse Handicap

runners.name: Fircombe Hall
runners.spotlight: Won twice at Wolverhampton (6f) last month and he was unfortunate not to finish closer when sixth over this C&D on his penultimate start; beaten a neck at Newcastle (6f, AW) three days ago and there's no reason why he can't go well again.
runners.comment: Unfortunate not to finish closer here on his penultimate start and ran well three days ago

runners.name: Raft Up
runners.spotlight: Placed behind prolific winner Yorkshire Glory at Newcastle on two occasions (February/March) before racing keenly and running below his best here over 7f 16 days ago; should be happier back at 6f.
runners.comment: Below his best having raced keenly here over 7f last time but should be happier back at 6f

runners.name: Valsharah
runners.spotlight: 21-race maiden but he was runner-up at Kempton (6f, AW; first-time tongue-tie) last month and posted another solid effort at Wolverhampton 19 days ago; has each-way claims.
runners.comment: 21-race maiden but his last two efforts have been solid; has each-way claims

runners.name: Moostar
runners.spotlight: One win from 12 starts but she's been back to form since the tongue-tie was fitted, second at Chelmsford and third at Wolverhampton in December before finishing runner-up on her return at Catterick (6f, good) 15 days ago; contender.
runners.comment: 1-12 but she's been in good form since the tongue-tie was fitted in December; contender

runners.name: Megaphone
runners.spotlight: Had beaten only one rival home in his first four starts for this stable but he took a step back in the right direction when third at Wolverhampton (6f, AW) six days ago when wearing the hood on its own (same today); chance.
runners.comment: Took step back in the right direction when third in just a hood at Wolverhampton last time

runners.name: Blackjack
runners.spotlight: Often front-runner; fourth of nine over C&D in January but he's struggled in three starts since; another headgear swap; others preferred.
runners.comment: Struggled in three starts since finishing fourth over C&D in January; others preferred
